chef_bio.philosophy: Rather than expanding into many flavors, the shop deliberately keeps to a single, authentic koshi-an momiji manju, baking fresh to order through the day with no leftover stock from the previous day, and keeping prices low (around 90 yen each).
chef_bio.specialty: Traditional hand-made koshi-an (smooth strained red bean paste) momiji manju — described as Miyajima's only shop dedicated solely to the koshi-an style, with a fluffy soft batter and lightly sweetened house-made bean paste.

anecdotes.text: The shop bakes its momiji manju fresh through the morning rather than selling pre-made stock; a visitor arriving around 10 a.m. found baking still in progress.
It is known as one of the most affordable momiji manju shops on Miyajima, at roughly 90 yen each, and offers discounts for school-trip students.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: もみじ饅頭データベース(momiji-manju.jp)が大正末(約1923年)創業、宮島唯一のこし餡専門店と記載。ブログが焼き立て・約90円の低価格・修学旅行割引を確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The shop is a wheat-flour momiji manju bakery, so the environment is inherently flour-heavy and not suitable for gluten avoidance.

cultural_comfort.note: A tiny old-fashioned family sweets stall on Miyajima's main street; little to no English, but ordering is simple point-and-pay (cash only), so a foreign student can easily buy a freshly baked momiji manju for about 90 yen.

places.name: そば処 とんぼ 宮島店 (Soba-dokoro Tonbo Miyajima-ten)
places.gf_detail: Not gluten-free. Soba noodles in Japan are typically blended with wheat flour (nihachi), the dipping/broth dashi uses wheat-based soy sauce, and the menu leans heavily on tempura (wheat batter) such as anago (eel) and shrimp tempura. No gluten-free preparation is advertised.
places.vegan_detail: Not vegan-friendly. Signature dishes include anago (conger eel) and shrimp tempura sets, meat soba, duck nanban soba, and steamed/grilled oysters, and soba broth is normally fish-dashi based with wheat soy sauce. No vegan options are documented.
places.neighborhood: Miyajima (Itsukushima) / Nakae, near Itsukushima Shrine
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A soba restaurant set inside a folk house on Miyajima reported to be over 100 years old, in the Nakae area a short walk from Itsukushima Shrine. The aged wooden building is part of the appeal, pairing the island's traditional streetscape with a sit-down noodle meal. (Map pin approximate — Omotesandō; use the Maps link for exact spot.)

chef_bio.background: A soba restaurant set inside a folk house on Miyajima reported to be over 100 years old, in the Nakae area a short walk from Itsukushima Shrine. The aged wooden building is part of the appeal, pairing the island's traditional streetscape with a sit-down noodle meal.
chef_bio.specialty: Soba served alongside hot, freshly fried tempura — including anago (conger eel) and shrimp tempura sets — plus regional touches like steamed oysters and deep-fried momiji manju for dessert. The zaru dipping sauce is described as aged for about two months in earthenware jars.

anecdotes.text: The shop occupies a reused folk house said to be more than 100 years old, with diners noting its Taisho/Showa-era character.
The zaru dipping sauce is reported to be carefully aged for about two months in earthenware jars, and tempura is served hot, fresh from the fryer.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログの口コミが築100年超の古民家再利用、穴子・海老天ぷら、蒸し牡蠣、揚げもみじ饅頭を確認(穴子は鱧の誤記なし)。つゆの約2か月熟成はrettyの口コミ由来として保持。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Menu centers on soba and tempura-based sets, which in Japan use wheat-based soy sauce and dashi for the broth and dipping sauce.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Signature dishes use fish, eel, duck, meat and oysters, and soba broth is typically fish-dashi based; no separate vegan prep is documented.

cultural_comfort.note: A small, casual tourist-area soba shop on Miyajima where a friendly greeting and pointing at the menu will get you by; do not expect English allergy support.

places.name: ＯＫ食堂 (OK Shokudo)
places.gf_detail: Not gluten-free friendly: chijimi (pajeon) pancakes are made from wheat-flour batter, Korean ramen is a wheat noodle, and the soy sauce and gochujang used in Korean cooking typically contain wheat. No gluten-free accommodations are documented.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centers on meat and seafood — grilled pork belly (samgyeopsal), marinated crab (gejang), and Korean ramen — and Korean staples here rely on wheat-based soy sauce and gochujang and egg-bound pancake (chijimi) batter, so there are no reliably vegan options documented.
places.hours_raw: One source indicates the kitchen serves until around 22:30; full daily hours and closed days are not published on the listings consulted.
places.neighborhood: 広島市中区田中町 (Naka-ku, east of the Nagarekawa/Yagenbori entertai
places.cuisine: Korean home cooking and set meals
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A small, casual neighborhood diner in central Hiroshima where the cooking leans into Korean home-style dishes despite the plain shokudo (canteen) name. Reviewers describe it as run by a Korean couple serving generous, everyday Korean fare alongside set-meal lunches.

chef_bio.background: A small, casual neighborhood diner in central Hiroshima (Tanaka-cho, Naka-ku) where the cooking leans into Korean home-style dishes despite the plain shokudo (canteen) name. Diners describe it as a family-run Korean kitchen serving generous, everyday Korean fare alongside set-meal lunches.
chef_bio.specialty: Korean comfort dishes — chijimi (pajeon) praised for a crispy outside and chewy inside, samgyeopsal (grilled pork belly), gejang (marinated crab), and Korean ramen, plus lunch set meals.

anecdotes.text: Diners single out the chijimi as a standout — crispy on the surface and chewy inside — with one calling it some of the most delicious Korean food they had had.
A lunchtime chijimi set (チヂミランチ) is a noted draw at midday.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 食べログがサムギョプサル・チヂミ・ケジャン・韓国ラーメン・日替わり定食を確認。運営者の氏名は非公開のため、経営者を『韓国人夫婦』と断定せず口コミ由来の家族経営として控えめに記載。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Korean cooking here uses standard soy sauce and gochujang, which typically contain wheat, and the pancake (chijimi) batter and Korean ramen are wheat-based.

cultural_comfort.note: A tiny, cash-only neighborhood spot run by what reviewers describe as a Korean couple with no documented English support, so a first-time foreign student would do best visiting with a Japanese- or Korean-speaking friend or a guide.

places.name: 広島 わなり (Hiroshima Wanari)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free provisions are documented. The kitchen leans heavily on wheat: Italian pasta and bread, fried oysters (kaki fry) and other breaded/fried items, plus Japanese dishes seasoned with regular (wheat-containing) soy sauce. No dedicated fryer or GF menu is mentioned anywhere; a celiac traveler should not rely on this place.
places.vegan_detail: This is a seafood- and meat-centered restaurant. Signature dishes are oysters (raw, grilled, fried), anago (conger eel), octopus, sashimi, T-bone steak, and game meat (venison, wild boar). Stocks and seasonings rely on fish/dashi and wheat soy sauce. There is no documented vegan menu; a strict vegan would find almost nothing suitable and should choose elsewhere.
places.hours_raw: Dinner 17:00–24:00 daily (holidays included). May close earlier when ingredients run out. Some aggregator listings additionally show morning/lunch service (9:00–14:30); the primary listing shows evening-only.
places.neighborhood: Otemachi, Naka-ku, Hiroshima (near Hondori and Fukuromachi)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] An independent, owner-run Hiroshima eatery in the Otemachi/Fukuro-machi area, a short walk from the Hondori shopping street. Despite a traditional Japanese-looking exterior, the kitchen is led by a trained Italian chef who previously ran a well-known local Italian resta

chef_bio.background: An independent, owner-run Hiroshima eatery in the Otemachi/Fukuro-machi area, a short walk from the Hondori shopping street. Despite a traditional Japanese-looking exterior, the kitchen is led by a chef with an Italian background who ran his own Italian restaurant for about twelve years before opening here; the owner's name is not publicly listed.
chef_bio.philosophy: Let prized Hiroshima land-and-sea ingredients shine, cooking them simply but expertly across two culinary traditions rather than confining the food to one style.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal Setouchi (Seto Inland Sea) seafood prepared in both Japanese and Italian styles: oysters sourced directly from producers in Kure (Ondo) and Osaki served raw, grilled, or fried; white-grilled local anago with sea salt and wasabi; grilled octopus; and hearty meat dishes like a signature T-bone steak and seasonal game.

anecdotes.text: Diners expecting straightforward Japanese washoku from the traditional signage are often surprised to find an Italian-trained chef, an extensive wine list, and T-bone steak alongside the oysters and anago.

The owner sometimes offers a generous off-menu set bowl only when the mood strikes him; one visitor was served a mushroom-and-chicken rice with salad, several small side dishes, pickles, and miso soup, the lineup changing with whatever is in stock.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: ヒトサラによると店主は呉市出身で、以前イタリアン店『夢跡人食道』を12年営んだ経歴。和食×イタリアン、呉(音戸)・大崎の牡蠣、穴子白焼き、Tボーンを確認。店主氏名は非公開のためchef_nameはnull。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Menu includes breaded/deep-fried oysters (kaki fry) and other fried items shared with wheat-based cooking; no dedicated gluten-free fryer or protocol is documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Japanese-style dishes use standard soy sauce, which contains wheat; no wheat-free seasoning is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen centers on seafood (oysters, anago, octopus, sashimi) and meat; vegetable dishes would share equipment and fish/dashi-based seasonings.

cultural_comfort.note: A relaxed local izakaya-style spot where some basic Japanese will help, since menus and the owner's off-menu daily specials are described mainly in Japanese; pointing at the seafood and a friendly attitude go a long way.

places.name: 餃子家 龍 新天地本店 (Gyozaya Ryu Shintenchi Honten)
places.gf_detail: This is a gyoza and Chinese izakaya, and gluten is everywhere here. Standard gyoza wrappers are made with wheat flour (the official site notes their pan-fried gyoza use 100% domestic wheat). Soy sauce, dipping sauces and most fried/seasoned dishes contain wheat. There is one "rice-flour water gyoza" (米粉水餃子) on the menu, but the broth and seasonings are not documented as gluten-free and the kitchen handles wheat throughout, so cross-contact is very likely. No gluten-free program is advertised. Not suitable for someone with celiac disease or strict gluten avoidance.
places.vegan_detail: A meat-and-seafood izakaya: the gyoza are filled with pork and Hiroshima ingredients, and signature dishes like mapo tofu and the chili-oil chicken are meat-based. Soy sauce and dashi-style seasonings are used throughout. A few sides could be vegetable-leaning (gyoza-skin chips with lemon pepper, certain vegetable plates), but nothing is confirmed vegan and there is no vegan/vegetarian menu. A vegan would have very few safe choices and should not rely on this place for a meal.
places.hours_raw: Sun-Thu & holidays: 16:00-00:00 (L.O. 23:30); Fri-Sat & day before holidays: 16:00-02:00 (L.O. 01:30). Closed Dec 31 and Jan 1.
places.neighborhood: Shintenchi, Naka-ku (near Hatchobori and Ebisucho)
places.notes: Gyozaya Ryu's Shintenchi honten (flagship) sits on Chuo-dori in the heart of Hiroshima's Shintenchi nightlife district, a few minutes' walk from Hatchobori. It has become one of the city's go-to gyoza spots, the kind of name locals reflexively suggest when asked where t

chef_bio.background: Gyozaya Ryu's Shintenchi honten (flagship) sits on Chuo-dori in the heart of Hiroshima's Shintenchi nightlife district, a few minutes' walk from Hatchobori. It has become one of the city's go-to gyoza spots, the kind of name locals reflexively suggest when asked where to eat dumplings.
chef_bio.philosophy: The shop wears its love of dumplings openly, with the slogan 'NO GYOZA, NO LIFE!' and a commitment to making gyoza fresh every day at a dedicated factory.
chef_bio.specialty: Hand-wrapped pan-fried gyoza made with domestic wheat flour and stuffed with pork and Hiroshima-area ingredients, delivered fresh daily from the group's own gyoza factory. The menu fans out into playful local riffs such as Carp gyoza (red-skinned, a nod to the hometown baseball team) and okonomiyaki-sauce gyoza, alongside Chinese izakaya staples like a Sichuan-pepper-forward mapo tofu and chili-oil chicken.

anecdotes.text: In a crowdsourced poll of dozens of Hiroshima diners naming their favorite gyoza shops, Gyozaya Ryu placed among the top picks, which is what drew one food blogger to visit.
The red-skinned 'Carp gyoza' is described as a team-approved item 'filled with meat juice and Carp love,' tying the shop to Hiroshima's beloved baseball club.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトが新天地本店(八丁堀近く)、『NO GYOZA, NO LIFE!』、自社餃子工場、赤い皮のカープ餃子(肉汁とカープ愛)、山椒の効いた麻婆豆腐を確認。

gf_cross_contamination.text: A wheat-heavy kitchen producing pan-fried wheat-flour gyoza, fried items and noodle-style dishes; gluten cross-contact is very likely and no gluten-free handling is advertised.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese/Chinese soy sauce and sauces are used across the menu (e.g., okonomiyaki-sauce gyoza); these contain wheat. No wheat-free soy sauce is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Gyoza are pork-filled and most dishes are meat- or seafood-based with soy/dashi seasonings cooked on shared equipment; no vegan preparation is documented.

cultural_comfort.note: A casual, lively local gyoza izakaya where orders are placed on a touchscreen panel and many payment methods (cards, IC, QR) are accepted, so a foreign student can manage comfortably with minimal Japanese, though the menu and staff are primarily Japanese.

places.name: ゆかりんの台所 (Yukarin no Daidokoro)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free accommodations are documented. This is an Italian-based bistro/izakaya whose menu centers on pasta, bread, fried items, and dishes plated with wheat-containing sauces; nothing on the official site or listing indicates a gluten-free menu or handling.
places.vegan_detail: No vegan or vegetarian program is documented. Signature dishes are meat- and seafood-forward (beef steak, Iberico chorizo, mussels in white wine, herb-butter beef). While they advertise fresh organic vegetables (e.g. whole roasted broccoli with garlic oil), there is no indication these are prepared without animal products, so a strictly vegan diner cannot be assured here without asking.
places.hours_raw: 18:00 - 02:00 (L.O. 01:00). Closed Tuesdays. Open year-end/New Year period 12/18-1/2. (Note: official site lists a temporary closure 5/13-15 and states normally open Tuesdays; the listing indicates Tuesday as the regular closed day.)
places.neighborhood: Kanayamacho / Hatchobori area, Naka-ku
places.notes: A casual, independently run Italian-based eatery in central Hiroshima near the Hatchobori/Kanayamacho area. It serves original Italian dishes in an unpretentious, everyday setting, with the owner personally selecting the wines.

chef_bio.background: A casual, independently run Italian-based eatery in central Hiroshima near the Hatchobori/Kanayamacho area. It serves original Italian dishes in an unpretentious, everyday setting, with the owner personally selecting the wines.
chef_bio.philosophy: Casual Italian dining without pretense, using fresh organic vegetables from contracted farmers and accessibly priced wines chosen by the owner.
chef_bio.specialty: Creative Italian home-style cooking such as a whole roasted broccoli with garlic oil, beef steak, Iberico chorizo with dried figs, and mussels in white wine, paired with barrel-aged self-serve wines (red, white, sparkling) kept fresh by a wine-serving system.

anecdotes.text: The shop installs a wine-serving system that protects barrel wine from air and light to keep its fresh taste, with self-serve barrel wine available to guests.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトが銀山町近く、イタリアンベースの独創料理、店主選定ワイン、契約農家の有機野菜、丸ごとローストブロッコリー・ビーフステーキ・イベリコチョリソーと干し無花果・ムール貝、樽ワインのセルフサーブ供給システムを確認。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Menu includes a herb-butter soy-flavored beef steak; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat, so soy-seasoned dishes here should be assumed to contain gluten.

positives.text: The kitchen uses fresh organic vegetables from contracted farmers, so plant-forward sides may be available on request.

cultural_comfort.note: A casual, owner-run Italian izakaya where ordering and signage are in Japanese; a foreign student comfortable with basic Japanese or a translation app will manage fine, and the relaxed atmosphere is welcoming.

places.name: 袋町ワイン食堂 LE JYAN JYAN (Fukuromachi Wine Shokudo LE JYAN JYAN (Le Jy)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free program is documented. This is a wine bistro built around hamburger steak, pates, oil-cured sardines and wine-friendly small plates, where wheat-based sauces, flour and standard soy sauce are common; nothing on the official site or listings indicates dedicated GF preparation. A GF diner would need to ask in advance and verify each dish.
places.vegan_detail: The kitchen genuinely emphasizes vegetables, using produce from contract and own farms, and a vegetarian menu is noted in listings, so plant-forward plates do exist. However, the house is meat- and seafood-centric (signature hamburger steak, oil sardines, pates) with no documented fully vegan dishes or vegan-cross-contact controls; butter, cheese, eggs and stock are likely in many vegetable dishes. A vegan should call ahead and confirm preparation.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 17:30–23:00 (L.O. 22:00); closed Sunday
places.neighborhood: Fukuro-cho, Naka-ku, near Hondori arcade (about 5 min from t
places.notes: A袋町 (Fukuro-cho) wine shokudo just off the Hondori arcade in central Hiroshima, set on the second floor of the Fukuro-cho Industrial Building. It bills itself as a grown-up wine tavern, the kind of place where you settle in at the counter or around a big shared oval tab

chef_bio.background: A袋町 (Fukuro-cho) wine shokudo just off the Hondori arcade in central Hiroshima, set on the second floor of the Fukuro-cho Industrial Building. It bills itself as a grown-up wine tavern, the kind of place where you settle in at the counter or around a big shared oval table for an unhurried evening.
chef_bio.philosophy: 肩ひじ張らず、ワインを愉しむ — enjoy wine without pretension. Rather than handing over a fixed wine list, the staff talk with guests to pour something that fits the mood and the food that night.
chef_bio.specialty: Around 250 affordably curated wines (many by the glass, rotating daily) paired with a vegetable-forward, wine-friendly kitchen. Standout plates include a hand-kneaded hamburger steak that has been on the menu since opening and house-made oil-cured sardines, with daily fish and vegetables displayed market-style at the counter.

anecdotes.text: The kitchen leans on vegetables from contract farms and its own farm, treating produce as a centerpiece alongside the wine rather than an afterthought.
The hand-kneaded hamburger steak and the house-made oil sardines have been signatures since the restaurant first opened, kept on the menu by loyal regulars.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式(owst)が袋町産業ビル2F、肩ひじ張らないワイン食堂、気分と料理に合わせた提案、開店以来の自家製オイルサーディン・手ごねハンバーグ、契約農家・自社農園の野菜を確認。ワイン250種以上。

positives.text: Kitchen makes a point of using fresh vegetables from contract and own farms, so plant-based plates are part of the everyday menu.

cultural_comfort.note: A relaxed evening wine spot where a few words of Japanese and pointing at the daily counter display of fish and vegetables will get you a warm welcome, though detailed allergy questions may need a translation app.

places.name: 広島らーめん たかひろ (Hiroshima Ramen Takahiro)
places.gf_detail: Not gluten-free. The ramen uses wheat noodles and a shoyu (wheat-based soy sauce) tonkotsu broth, and the oden and izakaya dishes also rely on soy sauce and other wheat-containing seasonings. No gluten-free menu or accommodation is documented.
places.vegan_detail: Not vegan-friendly. The signature ramen is built on a pork-bone (tonkotsu) broth with chashu pork, and oyster ramen and oden feature seafood and animal-based dashi. No vegan options are documented.
places.neighborhood: エキニシ (Ekinishi), 広島市南区大須賀町, near JR Hiroshima Station
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A small, independent ramen-and-oden counter shop tucked into the lively Ekinishi district just west of Hiroshima Station, a short walk from the platforms. With only seven seats at the counter downstairs and a small private room upstairs, it has the intimate feel of a ne

chef_bio.origin: Opened in its current Ekinishi location around August 1, 2016.
chef_bio.background: A small, independent ramen-and-oden counter shop tucked into the lively Ekinishi district just west of Hiroshima Station, a short (roughly 4-minute) walk from the platforms. With seven seats at the counter downstairs and a couple of small private rooms upstairs, it has the intimate feel of a neighborhood late-night spot where regulars and after-work drinkers gather, run as a small family operation by the owner and his daughter.
chef_bio.specialty: Hiroshima-style ramen in the nostalgic local mold: a soy-seasoned pork-and-chicken-bone (tonkotsu) broth topped simply with chashu, green onion, bean sprouts and menma. A winter-only oyster ramen puts the region's plump oysters and spinach into a creamy, buttery bowl. Oden and izakaya plates round out the menu, making it a popular finishing stop after drinks.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers describe the ramen as gentle and clean-tasting in the nostalgic Hiroshima style, light and easy to finish even after a night out, well suited as a closing dish.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog and Ekiten confirm the Ekinishi location near Hiroshima Station, a ~2016 opening, 7 counter seats plus 2F private rooms, a soy-seasoned pork-and-chicken-bone broth with standard toppings, and an owner-and-daughter operation; kakikuken documents the winter-limited oyster ramen with spinach in a creamy buttery soup.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The ramen is a Hiroshima-style shoyu (soy sauce) tonkotsu, and soy sauce in Japan is normally wheat-based; oden broth is also soy-seasoned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Broth is pork-bone based with chashu pork; oyster ramen and oden include seafood, so animal products are integral throughout the kitchen.

cultural_comfort.note: A tiny 7-seat counter spot run by locals where Japanese is expected; come with a few basic Japanese phrases or a translation app, and note it is cash-only.

places.name: 鉄板 ようじろう (Teppan Yojiro)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free offering. This is a teppanyaki/okonomiyaki izakaya: the okonomiyaki (ようじろう焼き, 広島スペシャル, 肉玉そば) is built on a wheat-flour batter and wheat noodles, and savory dishes lean on wheat-containing soy sauce and ponzu. Some naturally gluten-free items exist in principle (sashimi, grilled beef, oysters, grilled vegetables) but there is no documented gluten-free menu, no dedicated preparation area, and no stated allergy handling. A celiac or strongly gluten-sensitive diner should not rely on this restaurant.
places.vegan_detail: Not vegan-friendly. The menu is centered on fresh fish/sashimi (morning-caught Toyoshima fish), oysters, octopus, Sera beef steak/tataki, bacon and egg-based teppanyaki dishes, and okonomiyaki containing egg, pork and wheat. The few plant-based-looking items (grilled potato salad, mushroom butter) use dairy/butter and share the teppan grill with fish and meat. There is no documented vegan or vegetarian menu.
places.hours_raw: 17:00 - 02:00 (open daily, including holidays and pre-holiday days)
places.neighborhood: Shintenchi, Naka-ku (Nagarekawa/Hatchobori nightlife distric
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] 鉄板 ようじろう is a small, independently run teppanyaki and Hiroshima-okonomiyaki spot that opened on May 1, 2026, on the 4th floor of a building in the Shintenchi area of central Hiroshima, deep in the Nagarekawa nightlife district. The chef trained at established teppanyaki

chef_bio.background: 鉄板 ようじろう is a small, independently run teppanyaki and Hiroshima-okonomiyaki spot that opened on May 1, 2026, on the 4th floor of a building in the Shintenchi area of central Hiroshima, on the edge of the Nagarekawa nightlife district.
chef_bio.philosophy: An approachable counter-and-table spot where you can watch the cooking on the iron plate and taste Hiroshima specialties paired with local sake, in a relaxed izakaya atmosphere rather than a formal teppanyaki room.
chef_bio.specialty: Casual teppanyaki built around local Setouchi ingredients: morning-caught fish from Toyoshima served as sashimi, Hiroshima oysters prepared butter-sautéed, ponzu or ahijo-style, Sera 'minori' beef (世羅みのり牛) as tataki and steak, and house okonomiyaki including the signature ようじろう焼き.

anecdotes.text: The chef grills morning-caught Toyoshima fish and Sera 'minori' beef in front of guests, and turns out the house ようじろう焼き okonomiyaki alongside Hiroshima oysters done three ways.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Tabelog's listing and menu confirm the May 1, 2026 opening, the Shintenchi 4F location, Toyoshima morning-catch sashimi, Hiroshima oyster preparations (butter/ponzu/ahijo), 世羅みのり牛 tataki and steak, and the signature ようじろう焼き. No published source documents the chef's prior training, so that claim was removed.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Okonomiyaki uses a wheat-flour batter and wheat noodles, and is cooked on the same shared iron griddle as all other dishes, so gluten cross-contact on the teppan is effectively unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Savory teppanyaki and ponzu-dressed dishes (e.g., octopus with ponzu) rely on Japanese soy sauce / ponzu, which standardly contain wheat; no wheat-free seasoning is advertised.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Nearly all dishes involve fish, shellfish, beef, pork or egg, and plant items are cooked on the same teppan as seafood and meat; no separated vegan preparation is described.

cultural_comfort.note: This is a late-night local teppanyaki izakaya with counter and table seating where ordering is in Japanese; a foreign student comfortable with basic Japanese (or pointing at the menu) can enjoy it, but no English support is documented.

places.name: 川創 新天地店 (Kawasou Shintenchi-ten)
places.gf_detail: Not gluten-free. The okonomiyaki batter and noodles (soba/yakisoba) are wheat-based, and dishes are finished with wheat-containing okonomiyaki/teppanyaki sauces. No gluten-free menu or accommodation is documented.
places.vegan_detail: Not suitable for vegans. The menu centers on pork, egg, hormone/offal (パワフル焼き), and fish-based items, with wheat-and-soy okonomiyaki sauce and bonito/fish stock common in this style. No vegan options are documented.
places.hours_raw: Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat 18:00–03:00 (L.O. 02:30) / Sun, holidays 17:00–00:00 (L.O. 23:30) / closed Mon
places.neighborhood: Shintenchi / Nagarekawa-Yagenbori entertainment district, Na
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A small, independently run teppanyaki and okonomiyaki house tucked into Hiroshima's Shintenchi-Nagarekawa nightlife quarter, just off the bustle of Nagarekawa near Shintenchi Park. It runs late into the small hours, making it a go-to for a hot, sizzling meal after a nig

chef_bio.background: A small, independently run teppanyaki and okonomiyaki house tucked into Hiroshima's Shintenchi-Nagarekawa nightlife quarter, near Shintenchi Park in Mikawa-cho. It runs late into the small hours, making it a go-to for a hot, sizzling meal after a night out.
chef_bio.specialty: Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki built on the griddle, alongside an unusually broad teppanyaki lineup. Signature plates include the house 川創焼き (squid tempura, pork, shiso and green onion over thin noodles), hearty 肉玉そば, offal-rich パワフル焼き, and inventive griddle dishes like curry yakisoba and a charcoal-noodle 'macho' yaki.

anecdotes.text: Regulars treat it as a late-night refuge: it stays open well past midnight, energetically firing the griddle for diners coming off the drinking streets of Nagarekawa.

The kitchen leans into creativity beyond standard okonomiyaki — uni-and-spinach teppanyaki, butter-grilled squid and griddle potato salad — and pours Carp-brand sauce for a spicier edge, drawing notice from local food bloggers.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Park-side and asap blogs confirm the Mikawa-cho location near Shintenchi Park, late-night hours (Tue–Sat to ~4am), and dishes including 川創焼き, 肉玉そば, パワフル焼き, curry yakisoba and creative teppanyaki; a charcoal-noodle 'macho' yaki is noted as a past/occasional item.

cultural_comfort.note: A lively late-night local teppanyaki spot in the entertainment district where a little basic Japanese will help, as English support is unlikely and the vibe is casual and locally oriented.

places.name: 鉄心 (Tesshin)
places.gf_detail: This is a Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki and teppanyaki spot. The okonomiyaki batter and the noodles layered into it are wheat-based, and grilled/teppan dishes are typically seasoned with wheat-containing soy sauce and okonomiyaki sauce. No gluten-free menu, preparation, or accommodation is documented. Not suitable for celiacs or strict gluten avoiders.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centers on meat-and-noodle okonomiyaki, grilled skewers, oysters, beef tongue, and other animal-based teppanyaki dishes; okonomiyaki normally contains egg and the savory sauces are not vegan. No vegan or vegetarian dishes are documented. Not suitable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun & holidays 18:00–02:00 (food & drink L.O. 01:00); closed Mondays
places.neighborhood: Nagarekawa (Hatchobori / Nagarekawa nightlife district), Nak
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A casual teppanyaki and okonomiyaki spot tucked into the Nagarekawa nightlife quarter of central Hiroshima, the kind of place locals slip into after dark for the city's soul food. Cooks work the iron griddle right in front of guests, so the meal doubles as a bit of thea

chef_bio.background: A casual teppanyaki and okonomiyaki spot tucked into the Nagarekawa nightlife quarter of central Hiroshima, the kind of place locals slip into after dark for the city's soul food. Cooks work the iron griddle right in front of guests, so the meal doubles as a bit of theater.
chef_bio.philosophy: An emphasis on Hiroshima regional ingredients — noodles from local makers, regional vegetables, and Hiroshima specialties like oysters and beef tongue, paired with a deep list of Hiroshima sake.
chef_bio.specialty: Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki layered with noodles, plus teppan-grilled skewers and griddle dishes such as roasted tomato topped with melted cheese.

anecdotes.text: The drink list leans hard into Hiroshima's own breweries, pouring local sake such as Fukucho from Imada Shuzo in Akitsu, alongside Hoken and Miwazakura.
The counter seats directly facing the teppan are treated as the best seats in the house, where guests can chat with the friendly staff as the food is cooked.

cultural_comfort.note: This is a late-night local nightlife-district izakaya where staff cook on the teppan in front of you; English support is not documented and basic Japanese will make ordering much smoother, so it suits an adventurous student comfortable with a Japanese-language setting.

places.name: リンデン (Linden)
places.gf_detail: Tea-focused shop with over 100 varieties of black, flavored, and herbal teas, which are naturally gluten-free. However, the food side leans on wheat: the cafe is known for scones, brownies, macarons, and afternoon-tea pastries, all of which contain wheat flour. Lunch sets include rice-based, vegetable-forward dishes that may be more suitable, but no gluten-free menu or dedicated preparation is documented. A celiac or strongly gluten-sensitive guest would need to stick to plain tea and ask carefully about any food item; no allergen handling is published.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan establishment. Plain teas (black, flavored, herbal) are vegan, but milk is offered for tea. Lunch is described as vegetable-forward, but documented dishes include a shrimp and eggplant-tomato stew (not vegan), and the signature sweets (scones, brownies, macarons) typically contain dairy and eggs. There is no stated vegan menu. A vegan could likely enjoy a pot of plain tea, but should ask directly about any food item and milk-free options.
places.hours_raw: 11:00-20:00 (daily). Closed January 1st. (Some listings describe a daytime cafe 11:00-17:00 and evening cafe 17:00 onward, but the operating window is 11:00-20:00.)
places.neighborhood: Hondori, Naka-ku, Hiroshima (Hondori shopping arcade)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Linden is a small, independently run tea house tucked on the second floor of a building along Hiroshima's Hondori shopping arcade, a short walk from the city center. You climb a staircase to reach a simple, clean, light-filled room of small tables and a short counter, a

chef_bio.background: Linden is a small, independently run tea house tucked on the second floor of a building along Hiroshima's Hondori shopping arcade, a short walk from the city center. You climb a staircase to reach a simple, clean, light-filled room of small tables and a short counter, an unhurried space that welcomes solo visitors and pairs alike.
chef_bio.specialty: Tea is the heart of the place: the shop pours from a deep selection of black, flavored, and herbal teas, including its own original blends, and you can buy loose leaves to take home as well as drink in. Alongside the tea, the kitchen sends out afternoon-tea sets and light, vegetable-leaning lunches, with house sweets such as scones, matcha brownies, and macarons.

anecdotes.text: Visitors describe an impressively long tea list and order several pots in a sitting, each noticeably different in taste and aroma, paired with savory plates like a shrimp and eggplant-tomato stew with rice.
The room is praised as simple and clean, comfortable for both women and men, with warm, smiling service that makes the climb to the second floor worthwhile.

positives.text: Plain black, flavored, and herbal teas are served by the pot and are naturally free of gluten and animal products, making a tea-only visit straightforward.
Lunch options are described as vegetable-forward, which may give more flexibility for plant-based diners who ask about ingredients.

cultural_comfort.note: A calm, clean second-floor tea salon where staff are described as warm and friendly; a foreign student can comfortably order tea and snacks with a greeting and a few words or by pointing, though no English menu is documented.

places.name: メランジュ ドゥ シュハリ 広島 (MELANGE De SHUHARI Hiroshima)
places.gf_detail: Not gluten-free. The signature crepes and pancakes are made with French wheat flour, and macarons and cakes are flour-based as well. No gluten-free menu or handling is documented.
places.vegan_detail: No documented vegan offerings. The menu centers on crepes, pancakes, and macarons made with fermented butter, cream, eggs, and dairy, so the core items are not vegan, and the shop publishes no plant-based options.
places.hours_raw: 11:00-20:00 (food and drink last order 19:00), open daily
places.neighborhood: Hondori, Naka-ku, Hiroshima (Hondori shopping arcade)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A Hiroshima-born French-style cafe and patisserie on the Hontori shopping arcade, part of a small local group of MELANGE De SHUHARI shops that has grown to include a macaron takeout counter and a separate creperie nearby on the same arcade.

chef_bio.background: A Hiroshima-born French-style cafe and patisserie on the Hondori shopping arcade, part of a small local group of MELANGE De SHUHARI shops that has grown to include a macaron takeout counter and a separate creperie nearby on the same arcade.
chef_bio.specialty: Colorful macarons, made-to-order crepes and pancakes (using French wheat flour and AOP fermented butter), and seasonal cakes and afternoon-tea sets.

anecdotes.text: The signature crepe is built on a simple butter-and-sugar concept, with add-ons like raw chocolate, custard, whipped cream, and granola, and arrives more voluminous than expected after a short made-to-order wait.
The interior is a white-based, modern and refined space, designed for a leisurely afternoon over crepes, pancakes and tea.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The official site and Tabelog confirm three MELANGE De SHUHARI shops on Hondori (patisserie, macaron takeout, and a 3F creperie), the white-based modern interior, made-to-order crepes and pancakes using French wheat flour and AOP fermented butter, and the butter-and-sugar signature crepe.

cultural_comfort.note: A relaxed, walk-in-friendly café on the main Hontori arcade where a foreign student can comfortably order pastries and pancakes with a few basic Japanese words and pointing at the display.

places.name: こっから (Kokkara)
places.gf_detail: Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki is built on a wheat-flour crepe batter and finished with wheat-based okonomi sauce, and teppanyaki/izakaya dishes typically use wheat-containing soy sauce, so this is not a safe choice for gluten-free diners. No gluten-free accommodation is documented.
places.vegan_detail: The menu centers on Hiroshima okonomiyaki (egg, pork, often noodles), teppanyaki, horumon (offal), and seasonal items such as oyster dishes, all typically prepared with egg, meat, seafood, and fish/wheat-based sauces. No vegan options are documented and the kitchen is a shared teppan, so it is not suitable for vegans.
places.hours_raw: 16:00–02:00 daily (incl. holidays); irregular days off
places.neighborhood: Nagarekawa / Shintenchi (Horikawa-cho), Naka-ku
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki is built on a wheat-flour crepe batter and finished with wheat-based okonomi sauce, and teppanyaki/izakaya dishes typically use wheat-containing soy sauce, so this is not a safe choice for gluten-free diners. No gluten-free accommodation is d

chef_bio.background: Kokkara (こっから) is a Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki and teppanyaki shop on the 2nd floor of the Paris Building at 1-29 Horikawa-chō, Naka-ku, Hiroshima, in the Nagarekawa / Shintenchi nightlife district about a 2-minute walk from the Ebisu-chō tram stop. It cooks okonomiyaki and teppanyaki griddle dishes in the local layered style at a 16-seat counter. No official founding year, owner name or backstory is published on its listings.
chef_bio.specialty: Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki and teppanyaki cooked on the griddle.
chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese listings (Tabelog, Hitosara, Retty) confirm the Horikawa-chō location (Paris Building 2F) near the Ebisu-chō tram stop, a 16-seat counter, and the okonomiyaki/teppanyaki menu; there is no documented owner or founding date.

cultural_comfort.note: This is a tiny late-night counter okonomiyaki/teppanyaki spot in a busy nightlife district, so a little Japanese helps; staff are unlikely to speak much English and there is no English menu confirmed.

places.name: ほな (Hona)
places.gf_detail: This is a Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki and monjayaki shop. Okonomiyaki batter and the noodles, as well as monjayaki, are wheat-based, and the savory sauces typically contain wheat. There is no indication of gluten-free options or accommodation, so it is not suitable for those avoiding gluten.
places.vegan_detail: A traditional okonomiyaki/monja griddle shop where dishes typically include pork, egg, seafood, bonito-based dashi/toppings, and wheat-based savory sauce. No vegan or vegetarian offerings are documented, so it is not suitable for plant-based diners.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 18:00–04:00 / closed Sun
places.neighborhood: Yagenbori / Nagarekawa, Naka-ku
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] This is a Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki and monjayaki shop. Okonomiyaki batter and the noodles, as well as monjayaki, are wheat-based, and the savory sauces typically contain wheat. There is no indication of gluten-free options or accommodation, so it is not suitable for

chef_bio.background: Hona (ほな) is a Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki and monjayaki shop in the Yagenbori / Nagarekawa area of Naka-ku, Hiroshima, reached by tram to Ebisu-chō or Ginzan-chō and walking into the Yagenbori entertainment quarter. It serves Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki with fluffy cabbage and layered noodles, and some listings note a version topped with squid tempura (ika-ten). Dinner budgets are modest, roughly 1,000-2,000 yen per person. Published listings do not name the owner or give a founding date.
chef_bio.specialty: Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki (including an ika-tempura version) and monjayaki.

anecdotes.text: Listings describe a Hiroshima okonomiyaki with fluffy cabbage and noodles, with a squid-tempura (ika-ten) topping among its offerings.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese listings (Hot Pepper, Hitosara, Tabelog, Jalan) confirm the Yagenbori location and the okonomiyaki/monja menu with an ika-ten option, but provide no owner biography.

cultural_comfort.note: A tiny late-night neighborhood okonomiyaki and monja counter in the Nagarekawa nightlife district; little or no English is likely, so basic Japanese or a translation app will help a foreign student feel comfortable.

places.name: 麺麺 かわぐち (Menmen Kawaguchi)
places.gf_detail: This is a ramen and tsukemen shop where the core dish is wheat noodles, so the menu is fundamentally not gluten-free. Broths and tare typically contain wheat-based soy sauce and miso, and there is no indication of gluten-free options or accommodations.
places.vegan_detail: The shop centers on meat-based ramen and tsukemen with chashu pork, and broths for ramen and dipping soup are animal-based. There is no documented vegan or vegetarian option.
places.neighborhood: Nakamachi, Naka-ku (Fukuromachi/Tatemachi area, east of Peac
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] A small noodle shop that opened on December 3, 2024 on the second floor of a building in Nakamachi, central Hiroshima, a short walk east of Peace Memorial Park.

chef_bio.background: A small noodle shop that opened on December 3, 2024 on the second floor of a building in Nakamachi, central Hiroshima, a short walk east of Peace Memorial Park.
chef_bio.philosophy: The owner takes the noodles themselves seriously, sourcing lunch tsukemen noodles from Aichi and the evening miso ramen noodles from Hokkaido to suit each style of dish.
chef_bio.specialty: A two-faced noodle concept: at lunch the shop serves dipping-style tsukesoba, and at dinner it switches entirely to miso ramen, offering white, red, and black miso variations including a garlic miso butter bowl.

anecdotes.text: The lunch dipping noodles and the dinner miso ramen use different noodles sourced from different regions — Aichi for the lunch tsukemen and Hokkaido for the evening miso ramen.
The dual lunch-tsukemen, dinner-miso-ramen format gives regulars a reason to come back at different times of day to try both sides of the menu.

cultural_comfort.note: This is a small, casual owner-run noodle shop where ordering is straightforward; basic Japanese or pointing at the menu works well, but expect little to no English support.

noodles.notes: Thick, substantial tsukemen noodles served chilled and firmly bound; reviewers praise a pronounced wheat aroma and flavor with a smooth, slightly chewy (mochiri) surface that grips the dense dip well. Noodles sourced from a maker in Aichi. The dinner miso ramen uses a medium-thick chewy noodle.

ramen.signature_bowl: Tokusei tsukesoba (special tsukemen) - thick chilled noodles with a concentrated gyokai-tonkotsu (pork-and-seafood) dipping broth. The dinner-only garlic miso butter ramen is the other half of the shop's two-faced menu.
ramen.chashu: Generous thick-cut pork slices torched (aburi) to order for a charred, crisp surface, then warmed in the hot dip so the fat turns glossy and melting.

gf.note: Wheat tsukemen and ramen noodles plus a soy-based (wheat) shoyu dip mean these bowls are not celiac-safe; no gluten-free noodle or tare option is documented.

vegan.note: The dip is a pork-bone and seafood broth and the bowls feature pork chashu, so there is no plant-based option; fish and animal stock are integral to the soup.

ramen.pantheon: A concentrated pork-and-seafood (gyokai-tonkotsu) dipping broth places this bowl squarely in the lineage of modern Kanto-style noukou tsukemen rather than Hiroshima's chili-laced local style - a thick, sweet-edged, mildly tangy dip you taste the pork and fish dashi working together. The shop wears two faces: a rich tsukemen by day and a garlic-miso-butter ramen by night.

places.highlight: A concentrated pork-and-seafood (gyokai-tonkotsu) dipping broth places this bowl squarely in the lineage of modern Kanto-style noukou tsukemen rather than Hiroshima's chili-laced local style - a thick, sweet-edged, mildly tangy dip you taste the pork and fish dashi working together. The shop wears two faces: a rich tsukemen by day and a garlic-miso-butter ramen by night.

places.name: 麦家人 (Bakkajin)
places.gf_detail: The house specialty is wheat noodles made by the adjacent Harada Seimen, served in a soy-sauce broth (soy sauce contains wheat). This is a wheat-noodle shop with no gluten-free option.
places.vegan_detail: The cold noodles and tsukemen are built around a soy-sauce-based broth with pork chashu and other meat/fish-derived elements; no vegan offering is documented.
places.hours_raw: Tue/Thu/Sun/holidays 11:30-14:00; Wed/Fri/Sat 11:30-14:00 and 17:30-21:00; closed Monday; Sunday lunch served on alternating weeks only.
places.neighborhood: Eba Sakaemachi, Naka Ward (southwest-central Hiroshima)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened September 2021 in the EBBA VILLAGE building right beside the Harada Seimen noodle factory in Eba Sakaemachi. It was started by a relative of the Harada Seimen family, with the storefront serving customers and rental units occupying the back of the building.

roles: family-run noodle shop tied to Harada Seimen
chef_bio.background: Opened September 2021 in the EBBA VILLAGE building right beside the Harada Seimen (原田製麺) noodle factory in Eba Sakaemachi. It was started by a relative of the Harada Seimen family and runs as a friendly, no-frills counter shop.
chef_bio.philosophy: Showcasing the family's own Harada Seimen noodles in a friendly, no-frills counter setting.
chef_bio.specialty: Hiroshima cold noodles made with Harada Seimen's own wheat noodles, plus evening izakaya fare

anecdotes.text: Reviewers note the broth runs intensely savory and soy-sauce-forward; when one diner found it too salty, the smiling staff repeatedly offered to add more spice rather than dilute it.
A bowl runs about 1,150 yen, roughly 350 yen cheaper than the noodles made from the same Harada Seimen supply served at the well-known Shinkaen.
Visitors describe the kitchen as run by a few cheerful women whose friendliness is part of the draw.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: oomin77 and h-megourmet confirm the Sept 2021 opening beside Harada Seimen in Eba Sakaemachi, the Harada-family tie, Hiroshima cold noodles made with Harada Seimen noodles (~1,150 yen, cheaper than Shinkaen which uses the same supply), a strong soy-forward broth, a night izakaya format, and cheerful female staff.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The entire menu centers on wheat noodles produced at the adjacent Harada Seimen factory, so the kitchen is a heavy wheat-flour environment.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature broth is described as intensely soy-sauce-forward; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dishes are topped with pork chashu and served in a meat/fish-and-soy-based broth; no plant-only preparation is documented.

cultural_comfort.note: A small family-run noodle counter where ordering is straightforward but staff likely speak little English, so a few words of Japanese or pointing at the menu will help.

places.name: Baker theta (ベイカー シータ)
places.gf_detail: Baker theta is a 100% rice-flour bakery that uses no wheat flour at all; the venue and local press describe it as 「米粉100％の身体に優しいパン」 (100% rice-flour bread that is gentle on the body) and a グルテンフリーの米粉パン屋さん (gluten-free rice-flour bakery), with 小麦粉不使用 (no wheat flour used). Individual breads are additionally labelled 小麦不使用 (wheat-free) alongside egg/dairy flags, e.g. ガーリックフランス is 卵・乳・小麦不使用. Because the entire kitchen is rice-flour based there is no wheat on the premises to cross-contaminate, but one allergy-focused source advises 「コンタミについては訪問前に必ずお店に確認をお取りください」 (confirm cross-contamination directly with the shop before visiting), so a celiac should still confirm shared-equipment handling on arrival.
places.vegan_detail: Not a vegan shop — the range includes egg sandwiches (平飼い卵のサンド), dairy butter breads and cheese/mentaiko/chicken items — but there are clearly vegan choices. ガーリックフランス (garlic French) is labelled 卵・乳・小麦不使用・ヴィーガン (egg/dairy/wheat-free, vegan), a ヴィーガンバター (vegan butter) spread is sold, and among the rice-flour scones the chunk-chocolate and black-sesame-kinako varieties are 卵不使用・乳不使用 (egg- and dairy-free). Ask staff, as recipes vary by day.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sat 10:00–17:00; closed Sun & Mon
places.neighborhood: 上温品 (Kami-Nukushina), Higashi-ku, Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A 100% rice-flour (米粉100％) bakery in Higashi-ku with no wheat flour on the premises — all breads, sandwiches and scones are gluten-free, which is unusually safe for celiacs in Hiroshima. Many items are also egg- or dairy-free and each is individually labelled, so allergen info is easy to read. It is primarily a takeaway bakery with a couple of outdoor benches; there is no full dine-in kitchen. Still worth confirming shared-oven/equipment handling on the day, per the shop's own cross-contact advice.

chef_bio.background: Baker theta opened on 19 December 2022 in Kami-Nukushina, Higashi-ku, Hiroshima, as a specialist 米粉パン (rice-flour bread) bakery. The shop's stated concept is 「身体に優しい無添加食材や、卵・乳製品アレルギーのある方でも食べられるパン」 — additive-free, body-friendly bread that people with egg and dairy allergies can also eat — built on 米粉100％ (100% rice flour) rather than wheat. It operates as a small neighbourhood take-out bakery on a Tuesday–Saturday schedule (closed Sun/Mon). The owner-baker's name is not disclosed in available Japanese press or listings; the shop is present on Instagram as @theta.komekopan.
chef_bio.philosophy: 身体に優しい (gentle on the body): additive-free, 100% rice flour, and, per the shop's stated concept, accessible to people with egg, dairy and wheat allergies.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% rice-flour breads described by the shop as gluten-free — curry pan, manul (Korean garlic-cream) pan, soy-milk cream buns — and rice-flour baked sweets such as cookies and donuts.

anecdotes.text: Local press highlights the distinctively shaped 豆乳クリームパン (soy-milk cream bun), and the カレーパン and マヌルパン are repeatedly called out as the shop's most popular items.
Some items such as the melon-pan sell out quickly, and certain sandwiches like the shio-koji chicken sandwich are noted as made-to-order (すぐ作れます).

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: リビング広島Web, 米パンなび, peekaboo and Tabelog describe Baker theta as a 米粉100％ / グルテンフリー, additive-free rice-flour bakery in 東区上温品 catering to egg/dairy allergies; opening date 2022-12-19 and Tue–Sat hours (closed Sun/Mon) from Tabelog. Products include カレーパン, マヌルパン and 豆乳クリームパン. No source names the owner.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Allergy-focused source explicitly advises confirming cross-contamination with the shop before visiting: 「コンタミについては訪問前に必ずお店に確認をお取りください」.
The entire bakery is 米粉100％ with no wheat flour used (小麦粉不使用), so there is no wheat on the premises to cross-contaminate.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: This is a rice-flour bakery, not a restaurant; soy sauce is not a featured ingredient and no wheat-based seasoning claims were found. Savory items such as mentaiko French and shio-koji chicken should still be checked for wheat-containing sauces.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Egg (平飼い卵のサンド), dairy (butter breads, cheese items) and fish (明太フランス) are handled in the same bakery, so vegan customers should ask about shared surfaces.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Each product is individually labelled with allergen flags such as 卵不使用・乳不使用・小麦不使用 (egg/dairy/wheat-free), making allergen status easy to verify at the shop.

positives.text: 100% rice-flour bakery with no wheat flour used anywhere — 「米粉100％の身体に優しいパン」— which is unusually safe for celiacs.
Additive-free concept catering explicitly to egg and dairy allergies, with per-item allergen labelling.

places.name: Big Apple (ビッグアップル)
places.gf_detail: A fully plant-based Indian cafe with a genuine wheat-free curry claim: local press reports the daily curry is made 小麦粉のルーを使わず (with no wheat-flour roux), thickened instead with an 11-spice blend and beans (soy/adzuki/kidney/lentil/chickpea) served with rice. The three cakes are described as グルテンフリー (chocolate, handmade-tofu cheesecake, daily pound cake). However the shared kitchen also turns out clearly wheat-bearing items — vegetable momo dumplings (wheat wrapper), soy-meat karaage (typically wheat/starch coating), tempeh fries and luorou rice (soy sauce = wheat) — so this is options, not dedicated. Falafel is chickpea-based but is fried and cross-contact with battered items in a shared fryer is likely; confirm rice+curry or the GF cakes and ask about tamari and the fryer.
places.vegan_detail: 100% vegan establishment — every item is animal-product-free per Vegewel and Tabelog (どのメニューも動物性不使用). Founder Guru Maiko built the menu around Indian vegetarian/vegan cooking; the tofu cheesecake uses handmade tofu 'cheese' and desserts are dairy-free. No meat, fish, egg or dairy on the menu.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00 (may close early); irregular days off
places.neighborhood: 廿日市市大野 (Ōno, Hatsukaichi — Miyajima ferry-gateway area near Miyajimaguchi)
places.cuisine: Vegan Indian curry & cafe (falafel, tempeh, cakes)
places.notes: [GF] Fully vegan cafe a short taxi ride (~5 min) or 25-min walk from the Miyajima ferry at Miyajimaguchi — a handy plant-based stop. Safest celiac order is the daily curry with rice (owner uses no wheat-flour roux, 11-spice blend + beans) or the gluten-free cakes/chai. Avoid the momo dumplings, luorou rice (soy sauce), and battered fried items; ask about the fryer for falafel and whether tamari can replace soy sauce. Small (14 seats), irregular hours and early closes — check the Instagram/LINE before going.

chef_bio.background: Owner Guru Maiko (reported age 39) discovered vegan cooking through yoga and travelled to India four times over about ten years, where she was struck by how flavourful meat-free food could be. She married an Indian man she met on her travels and opened Big Apple with her husband in spring 2023 in Ōno, Hatsukaichi. The white-themed, casual eatery-style cafe centres on Indian vegetarian/vegan home cooking. Contact is via a LINE official account and Instagram rather than a full website.
chef_bio.philosophy: 『肉がなくても、しっかり味がついたおいしい料理が食べられる』 — that meat-free food can be fully satisfying and richly seasoned.
chef_bio.specialty: Daily bean curry built on an 11-spice blend and no wheat roux; Indian-influenced vegan plates and gluten-free cakes.

anecdotes.text: The daily curry pairs a rotating bean (soy, adzuki, kidney, lentil or chickpea) with an 11-spice blend chosen to complement that day's bean, and uses no wheat-flour roux.
Maiko visited India four times across roughly a decade and opened the cafe with her Indian husband after being inspired by the country's vegetarian cooking.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 廿日市の地域メディア nh-times.jp と Vegewel（日本語）が、オーナーのグル麻衣子さんの経歴・2023年春開業・カレーが小麦粉ルー不使用・グルテンフリーのケーキ・完全ヴィーガンであることを報じている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared kitchen also prepares wheat items (momo dumplings, likely-battered karaage/tempeh fries), so cross-contact with GF orders is possible; not a dedicated GF kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Luorou (braised) rice is a soy-sauce-based dish; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat. Confirm tamari before treating any soy-seasoned dish as GF.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Establishment is 100% vegan, so no animal-product cross-contact risk.

staff_allergy_handling.text: English-speaking staff per Vegewel; owner speaks to a wheat-free curry, but no formal allergen protocol is documented — verify GF handling in person.

positives.text: Local press states the curry uses no wheat-flour roux (小麦粉のルーを使わず), thickened with an 11-spice blend and beans.
Three cakes (chocolate, tofu cheesecake, daily pound cake) are described as gluten-free and dairy-free.

places.name: CoCoRoBA (米粉ぱん CoCoRoBA)
places.gf_detail: A 100% rice-flour bread specialist that bakes no wheat at all. The shop's own site states 「国産米粉のみを使用し、余計な添加物を含まないグルテンフリーのおいしいパン生地作りに取り組みました」 and markets itself as a 「完全グルテンフリー米粉パン専門店」, using only domestic rice flour (Hiroshima Koshihikari + Kumamoto Mizuho Chikara blend). Curated GF guide komeko-palette confirms the facility 「小麦粉を一切使用していない」, eliminating cross-contact from equipment/utensils — no shared wheat line. Celiac-safe for the plain breads; note that cheese and chocolate varieties contain dairy (乳) though all items remain wheat- and egg-free.
places.vegan_detail: All breads are wheat- and egg-free, and many plain breads (e.g. 食パン, メロンパン) are also dairy-free and effectively vegan. However cheese and chocolate varieties contain dairy, and savory toasts use tuna (ツナ) and mentaiko (明太) fish roe, so not everything is plant-based. Ask which of the day's items are dairy- and fish-free.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–17:00 (closes when sold out); closed Wednesdays (open if a holiday)
places.neighborhood: 佐伯区利松 (Toshimatsu, Saeki-ku), Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A wheat-free rice-flour bakery — the whole facility uses only domestic rice flour, so there is no wheat line to cross-contaminate. Safe for celiacs for the plain breads; cheese/chocolate items add dairy and savory toasts add fish, so confirm your specific item is dairy/fish-free if that matters. Opens 10:00 and sells out, so go early.

chef_bio.background: CoCoRoBA opened in November 2022 in Toshimatsu, Saeki-ku, Hiroshima, as a dedicated gluten-free rice-flour bread shop. It was built around the three major food allergies (wheat, egg, dairy), aiming to give allergy sufferers and health-conscious customers bread they can eat with confidence. The owner sources only domestic rice flour — a blend of Hiroshima-grown Koshihikari and Kumamoto's Mizuho Chikara variety developed for rice bread — and uses moshio sea salt from Kami-Kamagari island and sudaki unrefined cane sugar (素焚糖) from Amami Oshima, avoiding unnecessary additives. The shop runs both a storefront and a nationwide online BASE shop.
chef_bio.philosophy: Bread that people with wheat, egg and dairy allergies can eat safely, made from carefully chosen domestic rice flour without unnecessary additives.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% rice-flour gluten-free breads (shokupan, melon pan, cream pan, savory toasts)

anecdotes.text: Uses a two-variety rice-flour blend — Hiroshima Koshihikari plus Kumamoto Mizuho Chikara — chosen for texture while keeping domestic sourcing.
The facility uses absolutely no wheat flour, fundamentally eliminating cross-contamination risk from equipment and utensils.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 店の公式サイトは「完全グルテンフリー米粉パン専門店」を掲げ、国産米粉のみ・小麦・卵不使用と明記。素材は上蒲刈島の藻塩、奄美大島の素焚糖など。komeko-paletteは製造設備・調理器具からの小麦混入リスクを根本的に排除した完全な小麦不使用環境と説明。2022年11月、佐伯区利松にオープン。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Facility uses only domestic rice flour and no wheat flour at all, fundamentally eliminating cross-contamination from equipment and utensils.
Shop describes itself as a fully gluten-free rice-flour bread specialist (完全グルテンフリー米粉パン専門店).

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Bakery, not a restaurant; savory toasts (tuna, mentaiko garlic) may use seasonings/sauces — celiacs should confirm those specific items, though plain breads carry no wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cheese and chocolate varieties contain dairy; savory toasts contain fish. Plain breads are egg- and dairy-free.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Positioned explicitly for the three major food allergies (wheat, egg, dairy); allergy-aware operation.

positives.text: Entirely wheat-free premises — no wheat baking on site, the strongest cross-contact protection a bakery can offer.
Nationwide online shop lets celiacs order the same GF breads remotely.

places.name: Okonomiyaki Koguma (お好み焼き 仔ぐま)
places.gf_detail: A rare genuinely gluten-free HIROSHIMA-style okonomiyaki — the local wheat trap, since Hiroshima-yaki normally layers a wheat crêpe batter AND wheat soba/udon noodles. This 1970-founded shop developed a dedicated GF version (¥690) whose batter and noodles are wheat-free: the venue states 「小麦粉と小麦由来成分を含まない生地と麺を使っています」(uses batter and noodles containing no wheat flour or wheat-derived ingredients), and also offers a 7大アレルゲンフリー (seven-major-allergen-free, i.e. no wheat/egg/milk) type. CRITICAL cross-contact caveat: the shop cooks ordinary WHEAT okonomiyaki on the same teppan all day, so the GF/allergen-free versions are made to order and require phoning a reservation by the previous day (要予約) — discuss celiac needs and shared-grill risk when you book; kept at ASK for that reason.
places.vegan_detail: Okonomiyaki here is built on egg, pork and (for standard versions) bonito/sauce — not a vegan venue. No vegan options are advertised.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–19:00; closed Tuesdays
places.neighborhood: 楽々園 (Rakurakuen), Saeki-ku, Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF] Order the グルテンフリーお好み焼き (¥690) or the 7大アレルゲンフリー type — the batter and noodles are wheat-free, which is genuinely unusual for Hiroshima-yaki. You MUST reserve by the day before so they prep the GF batter/noodles; the same teppan also cooks wheat okonomiyaki, so explain celiac disease and ask about a cleaned/separated cooking area. Also does frozen nationwide mail-order of the GF version.

chef_bio.background: 仔ぐま was founded in 1970 (昭和45年) in Rakurakuen, Saeki-ku, a residential district of southwest Hiroshima, and is run by owner-chef Nakagawa Ken. It is a long-established neighbourhood Hiroshima-yaki shop that took the unusual step of developing allergen-conscious versions of its dish: a seven-major-allergen-free okonomiyaki launched in 2013, followed about two years later by a fully gluten-free version, and a low-carbohydrate version on 1 October 2017. The low-carb/allergen work was reported by local press and Otafuku Sauce's okonomiyaki column, and the shop even ran blood-glucose testing in cooperation with a hospital. The GF and low-carb lines are also sold frozen for nationwide delivery.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make Hiroshima's signature okonomiyaki accessible to people who normally cannot eat it — those with wheat/gluten intolerance, celiac disease, or diabetes — without giving up the layered Hiroshima-yaki form.
chef_bio.specialty: Gluten-free and seven-allergen-free Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki with wheat-free batter and noodles

anecdotes.text: Developed a 7-major-allergen-free okonomiyaki in 2013, then a gluten-free version about two years later (2015), and a low-carb version (16.6g carbs) on 1 October 2017.
The low-carb noodles are made mainly from okara and soybeans rather than wheat.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 店の公式サイト（hiroshimayaki.biz）と広島経済新聞、オタフクソースのコラムより：1970年創業、店主・中川健。2013年に7大アレルゲンフリー、2015年にグルテンフリー、2017年10月1日に低糖質のお好み焼きを開発。グルテンフリータイプは小麦粉と小麦由来成分を含まない生地と麺を使用。いずれも要予約。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The shop also cooks standard wheat-based okonomiyaki and wheat soba/udon on the same teppan, so shared-surface cross-contact is a real risk; the GF/allergen-free versions are made to order and require reservation by the previous day.
GF okonomiyaki uses batter and noodles free of wheat flour and wheat-derived ingredients.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Okonomiyaki sauce is typically wheat-based; confirm the GF/allergen-free set uses a wheat-free sauce when reserving.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner developed allergen-free and gluten-free menus and offers custom combinations based on a customer's specific allergies; blood-glucose testing was run with hospital cooperation for the low-carb version.

positives.text: One of very few shops in Japan offering a genuinely wheat-free Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki (batter + noodles), a dish that is otherwise a double wheat trap.

places.name: Kotonoha (コトノハ)
places.gf_detail: A vegan and gluten-free baked-sweets workshop that uses no wheat at all. The shop's own site states 「小麦粉・卵・乳製品・白砂糖を使わず、米粉を使った焼き菓子」 and bills itself as a 〜Vegan & Gluten-free 工房〜 making treats 「誰でも安心して食べられる」. Everything is built on rice flour with no wheat on the premises, so cross-contact risk is minimal. This is a sweets-only shop (muffins, chiffon/pound cakes, cookies), not a place for a meal.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely plant-based: no eggs, no dairy, no wheat, and no refined white sugar. A reviewer notes the treats are 「言われなければ制限に気づかない」— delicious enough that you wouldn't know about the dietary restrictions. All baked sweets are vegan; confirm drinks if ordering those.
places.hours_raw: Wed–Sat 10:00–16:00 (closes when sold out); closed Sun, Mon, Tue
places.neighborhood: 安佐南区上安 (Kamiyasu, Asaminami-ku), Hiroshima — near Astram Kamiyasu Station
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A vegan and gluten-free 工房 that bakes only with rice flour — no wheat, egg, dairy or white sugar anywhere on site, so cross-contact is minimal and it's celiac-safe. Sweets only (muffins, chiffon and pound cakes, cookies), not a meal stop. Open Wed–Sat 10:00–16:00 and sells out; about 5 min (342 m) from Astram Kamiyasu Station, 2 parking spaces.

chef_bio.background: Kotonoha opened on 3 December 2019 in Kamiyasu, Asaminami-ku, Hiroshima, in a space that had previously been a takoyaki restaurant. It is a small workshop-style shop making vegan, gluten-free baked sweets — muffins, chiffon and pound cakes, cookies and granola — using rice flour instead of wheat, and no eggs, dairy or refined white sugar. The owner's stated goal is to make 「誰でも安心して食べられるおやつ」— snacks that anyone can eat with peace of mind, including people with allergies. The shop also ships nationwide, with free shipping over ¥4,500.
chef_bio.philosophy: Treats gentle on body and mind that anyone can eat safely — no wheat, egg, dairy or refined white sugar.
chef_bio.specialty: Vegan gluten-free rice-flour baked sweets (weekly-rotating muffins, chiffon/pound cakes, cookies)

anecdotes.text: Opened December 2019 in a former takoyaki restaurant space in Kamiyasu.
A reviewer said the sweets are so good you wouldn't know they are free of wheat, egg and dairy unless told.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイトは「小麦粉・卵・乳製品・白砂糖を使わず、米粉を使った焼き菓子」の Vegan & Gluten-free 工房と明記。2019年12月、安佐南区上安（旧たこ焼き店の跡地）にオープン。水〜土10〜16時営業、売り切れ次第閉店。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Bakes only with rice flour; no wheat flour used anywhere, so cross-contact risk in the workshop is minimal.
Self-described Vegan & Gluten-free 工房 making sweets without wheat flour, egg, dairy or white sugar.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets-only shop; no soy sauce / savory wheat seasonings involved.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fully plant-based — no eggs, dairy or animal products in the baked goods.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Mission is snacks anyone can eat safely, including allergy sufferers; featured by allergy-focused guide alleco.

positives.text: Entirely wheat-free premises using only rice flour — strong protection against gluten cross-contact.
Nationwide shipping (free over ¥4,500) for the same vegan GF sweets.

places.name: Maimaiko (まいまいこ)
places.gf_detail: A 100% rice-flour bakery and sweets shop that uses no wheat at all. The shop's own listing states 「100％米粉(グルテンフリー)のお菓子とパンの店です。小麦粉アレルギーの方に配慮しており、小麦粉は一切使用しておりません。厨房の調理過程で小麦粉が混入することもありません」 (a 100% rice-flour gluten-free sweets and bread shop; considerate of those with wheat allergy; no wheat flour used at all; no wheat flour contamination during the kitchen preparation process). No evidence the shop also bakes wheat or serves barley beer, so it reads as a dedicated GF kitchen. Takeout only; naturally low cross-contact risk, though celiacs may still wish to confirm shared equipment/ingredient sourcing directly.
places.vegan_detail: No vegan claims are published, and the core lineup (rice-flour chiffon cakes, florentine, buttery baked goods) typically contains egg and dairy. Some items such as the basil crackers or 'kimagure' cookie sets may be plant-based, but this is unconfirmed — vegans should ask the shop about each item.
places.hours_raw: Fri/Sat/Sun & public holidays 10:00–17:00 (or until sold out). Closed Mon–Thu (and Mon–Thu holidays). Takeout only.
places.neighborhood: 草津東 (Kusatsu-higashi), Nishi-ku, Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A 100% rice-flour (米粉) bakery in Kusatsu-higashi that uses zero wheat and states there is no wheat contamination during kitchen prep — a genuinely celiac-safe dedicated GF shop. Takeout only, open just Fri/Sat/Sun (plus holidays) 10:00–17:00, and popular items sell out, so arrive early. Products include rice-flour loaves, chiffon cakes, florentine, crackers and cookies; most contain egg/dairy so not vegan-safe.

chef_bio.background: Maimaiko opened in March 2022 in Kusatsu-higashi, Nishi-ku, Hiroshima, as a shop dedicated to 100% rice-flour (gluten-free) sweets and bread. It was founded with consideration for people with wheat allergy, using no wheat flour at all and preventing wheat contamination in the kitchen. The owner also runs a delivery arrangement called 'Maimaiko-bin' (まいまいこ便) through the shop Atelier Shion in Naka-ku, created in response to customers who wanted the rice-flour bread but could not travel out to Kusatsu. The shop is takeout-only and open only Friday through Sunday. The owner's name is not published in the available Japanese sources.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make bread and sweets that people with wheat allergy or celiac can eat safely, using only rice flour and keeping the kitchen entirely free of wheat.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% rice-flour bread (crispy outside, mochi-chewy inside) and rice-flour chiffon cakes

anecdotes.text: The 'Maimaiko-bin' delivery service was born from customers saying they wanted the rice-flour bread but couldn't make it out to Kusatsu: 「米粉のパンが食べたいけれど、草津までは…という方のお声をいただいて生まれた企画です」
The shop offers a rice-flour whole-cake decoration experience in addition to its regular takeout lineup.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: エキテン店舗ページによれば、まいまいこは2022年3月開店、広島市西区草津東の『100％米粉(グルテンフリー)のお菓子とパンの店』で、小麦粉は一切不使用、厨房での小麦粉混入もなし、小麦以外の食物アレルギーにも対応可能。あとりえしおんのページでは、草津まで来られない人向けの『まいまいこ便』受け取りサービスと、米粉シフォンケーキ・米粉パン・焼き菓子の価格が確認できる。bm-peekabooには米粉ホールケーキのデコレーション体験の記載あり。オーナー名の記載は見当たらない。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「小麦粉は一切使用しておりません。厨房の調理過程で小麦粉が混入することもありません」 — no wheat flour is used at all, and there is no wheat flour contamination during the kitchen preparation process.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Not applicable — this is a rice-flour bread and sweets bakery with no soy-sauce-based savory dishes; the concern here is wheat flour in baking, which the shop excludes entirely.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Core items such as rice-flour chiffon cakes and florentine contain egg and dairy; no vegan-dedicated equipment or claims are published.

staff_allergy_handling.text: 「小麦粉アレルギーの方に配慮しており」 and 「小麦以外の食べ物アレルギーにも対応可能」 — the shop is considerate of wheat-allergy customers and states it can also accommodate non-wheat food allergies.

positives.text: Fully dedicated 100% rice-flour (グルテンフリー) bakery — no wheat used anywhere and explicit no-cross-contamination claim, making it suitable for celiacs.
Corroborated as a 米粉パン・スイーツ specialty shop by an independent Hiroshima gluten-free roundup.

places.name: maisondecloth +cafe (メゾンドクロスカフェ)
places.gf_detail: A small gluten-free café that bakes rice-flour (米粉) bread and sweets. Its Instagram bio brands it 「グルテンフリーカフェ/米粉パン」 (gluten-free café / rice-flour bread), and Hiroshima food blogs describe it as 「グルテンフリーのカフェ」 serving 「グルテンフリーのスイーツ」. No wheat-flour items are documented on the menu, consistent with a whole-shop rice-flour concept. Caveat: the sources do not publish an explicit allergen/cross-contamination statement, so a celiac should confirm on booking that the entire menu — including any deli/lunch components and seasonings (soy sauce, miso) — is wheat-free.
places.vegan_detail: The café focuses on gluten-free rather than vegan; the signature lunch is a rice-flour waffle with 麹 (koji) deli sides, which may include dairy/egg. No vegan labeling is published — ask when reserving.
places.hours_raw: Thu–Sat 12:00–17:00 (some sources list Fri & Sat only, 13:00–17:00); small, irregular schedule — confirm via Instagram/phone
places.neighborhood: 可部南 (Kabe-minami), 安佐北区 (Asakita-ku), Hiroshima
places.cuisine: Gluten-free rice-flour café — bread, sweets, koji deli lunch
places.notes: [GF] Tiny 7-seat gluten-free café in northern Hiroshima (Kabe-minami, Asakita-ku) — a real trek from central Hiroshima and open only a few days a week (Thu–Sat), so reserve ahead by phone (080-1925-6839) or Instagram DM. Rice-flour bread is sold when available. No published dedicated-facility/cross-contact statement, so confirm whole-menu wheat-free scope directly.

chef_bio.background: maisondecloth +cafe is an owner-run gluten-free café in the Kabe-minami district of Hiroshima's northern Asakita ward, combining a small 7-seat café with a sale of rice-flour bread and stylish miscellaneous goods (雑貨). It operates only a few days a week (Thursday–Saturday) and was reviewed by Hiroshima food blogs in April 2025. The 'cloth' in the name and the accompanying goods shop point to a maker/craft background, but no owner name or detailed history is published in the available Japanese sources.
chef_bio.philosophy: Offer gluten-free, rice-flour-based bread and sweets in an intimate, shoe-free café where guests can 'forget the time'.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour bread and gluten-free sweets; rice-flour waffle with koji deli lunch.

anecdotes.text: 「木金土のみ営業するグルテンフリーのカフェ。全7席と小さいお店なので予約がベター」 — a gluten-free café open Thu/Fri/Sat only; with just 7 seats, reservation is recommended.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Instagram bio and two Hiroshima food blogs (modokimaru.com, bm-peekaboo.com) confirm a small gluten-free / rice-flour café in Kabe-minami, Asakita-ku, open 木金土のみ with 7 seats, selling rice-flour bread and gluten-free sweets, with a koji deli lunch and rice-flour waffle. No owner name is disclosed.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Branded a gluten-free / rice-flour café with no documented wheat items, but no explicit cross-contamination/allergen policy is published — confirm whole-menu wheat-free scope on reservation.

positives.text: Instagram bio: 「グルテンフリーカフェ/米粉パン」 — gluten-free café / rice-flour bread.
「グルテンフリーのスイーツが楽しめるカフェ」 — a café where you can enjoy gluten-free sweets.

places.name: Miyajima Guest House Mikuniya (宮島ゲストハウス三國屋)
places.gf_detail: A guesthouse a few minutes' walk from Itsukushima Shrine that serves its own original vegan curry for dinner and can accommodate gluten-free on request. The venue states 「動物性食材は一切使用しておりません…グルテンフリーにも対応可能ですので、ご希望の方は事前にお知らせください」(no animal ingredients used at all; gluten-free can also be accommodated, so please let us know in advance). Gluten-free is by advance notice only — standard Japanese curry roux and soy sauce are usually wheat-based — so confirm the roux, thickener and any soy sauce are wheat-free when you book, and note the shared self-service breakfast (bread, cereal) is not GF. Kept at ASK because GF relies on advance arrangement rather than a dedicated setup.
places.vegan_detail: The signature dinner is an original curry that the venue explicitly states uses no animal-derived ingredients whatsoever (動物性食材は一切使用しておりません), so it is fully plant-based. Breakfast is a self-service spread (bread, cereal, oatmeal, soup) that is not specifically vegan. The vegan offer centres on the dinner curry.
places.hours_raw: Guest meals: dinner 18:30–20:30, breakfast 05:30–09:30 (self-service; for staying guests)
places.neighborhood: 宮島町 (Miyajima / Itsukushima island), Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF] One of the very few gluten-free-accommodating dining options actually ON Miyajima island, where evening/morning food is scarce. Book dinner as a guest and give advance notice for gluten-free; the base dish is a wholly plant-based original curry, so confirm the curry roux, any thickener/flour and soy sauce are wheat-free (Japanese curry roux and soy sauce normally contain wheat). Self-service breakfast includes bread, so bring your own GF food for the morning.

chef_bio.background: 宮島ゲストハウス三國屋 is a small guesthouse on Miyajima island at 宮島町327, roughly a five-minute walk from Itsukushima Shrine. Because the island has very few restaurants open in the early morning and evening, the guesthouse provides its own meal service to staying guests: a self-service dinner built around an original curry (18:30–20:30) and a complimentary self-service breakfast (05:30–09:30). The kitchen's stated point of difference is that the curry uses no animal-derived ingredients and that gluten-free can be accommodated on advance request, which is unusual for the island.
chef_bio.philosophy: Provide reliable, diet-friendly meals for guests on an island where dining after dark or before dawn is otherwise hard to find.
chef_bio.specialty: Original all-plant-based (vegan) curry, gluten-free on request

anecdotes.text: The guesthouse notes Miyajima has very few restaurants open in the morning and evening, which is why it runs its own meal service.
Dinner is self-service and centred on an original curry containing no animal ingredients; gluten-free is accommodated when guests notify the house in advance.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト（miyajimamikuniya.com/meals）より：宮島町327、厳島神社徒歩数分のゲストハウス。夕食（18:30-20:30）はオリジナルカレーで「動物性食材は一切使用しておりません」。「グルテンフリーにも対応可能ですので、ご希望の方は事前にお知らせください」。朝食（05:30-09:30）はセルフサービス。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Gluten-free is accommodated only on advance request rather than by default; the standard dinner curry may use wheat-based roux/thickener, so confirm a wheat-free preparation when booking.
Self-service breakfast includes bread and cereal, presenting shared-surface gluten exposure at the morning buffet.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Japanese curry seasoning and soy sauce usually contain wheat; ask that the vegan curry be made with wheat-free/tamari seasoning for a gluten-free order.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The dinner curry is stated to contain no animal-derived ingredients at all (動物性食材は一切使用しておりません).

staff_allergy_handling.text: The house explicitly invites guests needing gluten-free to notify them in advance so they can accommodate it.

positives.text: One of very few gluten-free-accommodating and fully vegan dinner options actually on Miyajima island, where evening dining is scarce.

places.name: Taiko Ramen (太閤らーめん)
places.gf_detail: A Tabelog reviewer confirms you can swap the standard 全粒粉麺 (whole-wheat noodle) for a 米粉麺 (rice-flour noodle) as a グルテンフリー麺 substitution, and the kitchen advertises no added amino-acid seasoning or hydrolyzed protein. However, the rice noodle is boiled in the SAME water used for wheat noodles, and both the 塩 (salt) and 醤油 (soy) tare/broth are a fish-and-pork 'double soup' finished with tare that almost certainly contains wheat-based soy sauce. The shop makes no wheat-free or celiac claim of its own. Treat the rice-noodle bowl as reduced-gluten at best, NOT celiac-safe — shared boiling water and a wheat-soy tare mean real cross-contact and hidden-gluten risk.
