places.vegan_detail: Some aggregator listings and an English Tabelog summary state the shop can accommodate vegetarians/vegans, but no dedicated vegan bowl is confirmed on the menu, and the signature broth is a fish-and-pork double soup (non-vegan). Any plant-based option would have to be requested and verified in person.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00 (lunch), 18:00–21:00 (dinner, limited to 200 servings/day); irregular days off. Some listings show dinner ending 20:45.
places.neighborhood: 立町 (Tatemachi), Naka-ku, Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF] Ask before ordering: the 米粉麺 rice-noodle swap exists but is NOT celiac-safe. The noodle is boiled in shared water with wheat noodles, and the salt/soy tare and double-soup broth use wheat soy sauce. Cash/ticket-machine only, no reservations. Celiacs should treat this as a reduced-gluten novelty, not a safe meal; if strictly gluten-free, this shop cannot currently guarantee it.

chef_bio.background: 太閤らーめん is a spin-off (sister shop) of 太閤うどん (Taiko Udon), a well-known queue-forming udon shop in Hiroshima. The udon shop ran a popular once-a-month ramen special, and demand led the owners to open a dedicated ramen shop in Tatemachi (Naka-ku) so the ramen could be eaten any day. The shop opened on 30 January 2023. It emphasizes an additive-conscious kitchen (no added amino-acid seasoning, no hydrolyzed vegetable protein) and a light fish-and-pork double-soup dashi.
chef_bio.philosophy: Clean, additive-light broth built on Japanese dashi rather than chemical seasoning; a refined, approachable bowl.
chef_bio.specialty: 醤油らぁ麺 (soy-sauce ramen) and 塩らぁ麺 (salt ramen) with a fish-and-pork double soup; house whole-wheat noodles with an optional rice-flour noodle.

anecdotes.text: Opened as an offshoot of 太閤うどん so fans could get the shop's once-a-month ramen special any day of the week.
A January 2026 Tabelog reviewer ordered the salt ramen and swapped to the gluten-free 米粉麺 rice noodle, describing it as smooth and easy to eat.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト(taiko-noodle.com)は太閤うどんの姉妹店として2023年1月30日開店、醤油/塩らぁ麺(各1,100円)を提供と記載。食べログのレビュー(2026年1月)は通常の全粒粉麺をグルテンフリーの米粉麺に変更できると記録し、スープは魚介と豚のダブルスープ、アミノ酸添加物・タンパク加水分解物不使用と説明。米粉麺の茹で湯や醤油タレの小麦については保証なし。

gf_cross_contamination.text: The gluten-free 米粉麺 rice noodle is a swap on the standard menu; ramen kitchens boil all noodles in one shared pot, so the rice noodle is cooked in water contaminated by wheat noodles.
No dedicated gluten-free preparation or celiac protocol is claimed anywhere on the shop's own site or listings.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The signature bowls are 醤油らぁ麺 (soy-sauce ramen) and 塩らぁ麺 (salt ramen); Japanese ramen tare of this style is finished with wheat-based soy sauce, and no tamari/gluten-free soy sauce is advertised.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The base broth is a fish-and-pork double soup, so any vegetarian/vegan request would share equipment and stock area with animal broths; no separate vegan line is confirmed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Ordering is via ticket vending machine with no reservations; no documented allergen handling or celiac accommodation beyond the noodle swap.

positives.text: Kitchen advertises no added amino-acid seasoning and no hydrolyzed vegetable protein, and a rice-flour noodle can be requested in place of the wheat noodle.

places.name: Tamari Ya RICE DONUT (田万里家 ライスドーナツ ekie広島駅店)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated rice-flour (米粉) donut specialist; no wheat is used anywhere in the batter. The shop's own site states 「生地は小麦を使用せず、全てグルテンフリーです」 (the dough uses no wheat and is entirely gluten-free), and the ekie-opening press release repeats 「生地は小麦を使用せず全てグルテンフリー」. Because the entire product line is rice-flour based, facility-wide wheat cross-contact risk is low. Caveat: donuts are deep-fried and no dedicated-fryer or shared-oil policy is published — since it is a single-product rice-donut shop the fryer is almost certainly wheat-free, but a celiac should confirm on the day. Note also that the dough uses soybeans (大豆) — a soy allergen, not wheat.
places.vegan_detail: Eggs are not used in any donut (「卵は使用しておりません」). Plain varieties (米 fermented, 田 old-fashioned, 蕾 kinako) are dairy-free and effectively vegan. However chocolate-topped varieties contain dairy (「トッピングにチョコレートを使用しているものは、乳成分が含まれています」), so they are not vegan.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–21:00 (follows ekie Hiroshima Station opening hours)
places.neighborhood: ekie KITCHEN 1F, 広島駅 (Hiroshima Station), 南区, Hiroshima
places.notes: [GF] Convenient takeaway counter right inside Hiroshima Station (ekie KITCHEN, 1F) — ideal grab-and-go for celiac travelers. Every donut is rice-flour and wheat-free by the shop's own statement, and all are egg-free. Fried in rice-bran oil; no published dedicated-fryer policy but the shop makes only rice donuts, so shared-with-wheat oil is unlikely. Dough contains soy; chocolate-topped flavors contain dairy.

chef_bio.background: 田万里家 (Tamari-Ya) began as a farm-stay and rice producer in the depopulated hamlet of Tamari in Takehara, Hiroshima, growing pesticide-free rice on its own fields. To use its rice and revitalize the limited-population village (限界集落), it launched a rice-flour donut specialty business. On 25 April 2025 it opened the ekie広島駅店 counter inside Hiroshima Station's ekie KITCHEN, expanding from its rural base to a high-traffic station location.
chef_bio.philosophy: Use the farm's own pesticide-free rice to make additive-conscious, gluten-free sweets while supporting a shrinking rural community; no preservatives or artificial coloring, oxidation-resistant rice-bran oil, and organic toppings where possible.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour donuts — chewy fermented (発酵) donuts and old-fashioned donuts.

anecdotes.text: The company is described as a producer operating a rice-flour donut business rooted in a limited-population village (限界集落), which opened the ekie Hiroshima Station branch on 25 April 2025.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: prtimes.jp と straightpress、tamari-ya.com より：竹原市田万里（高齢化率の高い限界集落）で自家栽培の無農薬米を使う米農家が、無添加・グルテンフリーの米粉ドーナツ（発酵ドーナツ、オールドファッション）を製造。保存料・着色料不使用、酸化しにくい油、可能な範囲でオーガニックのトッピング。ekie広島駅店は2025年4月25日オープン。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No wheat is used in any product; the shop is a rice-flour donut specialist, so facility-wide wheat cross-contact is low. No published dedicated-fryer statement — confirm shared-oil status on the day.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Chocolate-topped varieties contain dairy (乳成分); plain varieties are dairy-free.

positives.text: 「生地は小麦を使用せず、全てグルテンフリーです」 — dough uses no wheat and is entirely gluten-free; eggs are not used.
ekie press release: 「生地は小麦を使用せず全てグルテンフリー」, additive-free, rice-bran oil, pesticide-free own rice.

places.name: comecome (こめこめ)
places.gf_detail: comecome is a 米粉のシフォンケーキ屋 (rice-flour chiffon cake shop) working out of a renovated house; its cakes are made with 米粉 (rice flour), きび砂糖 (unrefined cane sugar), 有機豆乳 (organic soy milk) and 米油 (rice oil) — the venue's own Japanese description is "米粉の他に、きび砂糖や有機豆乳、米油などこだわりの食材で作られた…家庭的なシフォンケーキ", with no wheat flour named. However, I could not locate an explicit venue-level 小麦不使用 / グルテンフリー / celiac claim, and no cross-contact protocol is published. Treat as a rice-flour-only kitchen that is gluten-free by product design but unverified for a celiac safety protocol — a celiac should confirm no wheat is handled anywhere and ask about baking powder / shared equipment before eating.
places.vegan_detail: The cakes are dairy-free — they use 有機豆乳 (organic soy milk) and 米油 (rice oil) rather than milk and butter. But chiffon cake is structurally egg-based (whipped egg whites), so these are not vegan and no vegan option was found.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–16:00, closed Sun–Fri (open Saturdays only)
places.neighborhood: 峰高 (Minetaka), Hatsukaichi
places.cuisine: Rice-flour chiffon cake specialty shop (sweets / takeout)
places.notes: [GF] Home-based rice-flour (米粉) chiffon cake specialty shop in Minetaka, inland Hatsukaichi near 峰高公園. Everything is built on rice flour with organic soy milk, rice oil and unrefined cane sugar — gluten-free by product, and no wheat items were found — but the shop publishes no celiac/cross-contact protocol, so this is 'high', not 'dedicated'. Open Saturdays only, ~11:00–16:00; flavors rotate and are announced on Instagram, so check before going and expect it to sell out. Cakes contain egg (chiffon), so dairy-free but not vegan.

chef_bio.background: comecome operates from a renovated single-family house (一軒家を改装した店内) in the Minetaka district of Hatsukaichi, on the inland Miyajima-line side of the city. The shop is a 米粉のシフォンケーキ屋 — a rice-flour chiffon cake specialist — and its cases are lined with homestyle chiffon cakes made from carefully chosen ingredients: rice flour plus unrefined cane sugar (きび砂糖), organic soy milk (有機豆乳) and rice oil (米油). Popular flavors and availability are announced on the shop's Instagram (@comecome_chiffon_). The owner's name and personal history are not published in the sources reviewed.
chef_bio.philosophy: Homestyle, 'made with love' (愛情のこもった) baking centered on rice flour and carefully selected, dairy-free ingredients (organic soy milk, rice oil, unrefined cane sugar).
chef_bio.specialty: 米粉シフォンケーキ (rice-flour chiffon cakes)

anecdotes.text: The shop is housed in a converted private house, and its rice-flour chiffon cakes use organic soy milk and rice oil instead of dairy and butter.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: bm-peekaboo の広島米粉スイーツ12選（#5）に「一軒家を改装したという店内には、米粉の他に、きび砂糖や有機豆乳、米油などこだわりの食材で作られた、愛情のこもった家庭的なシフォンケーキがずらりと並びます」と紹介。住所は広島県廿日市市峰高、営業時間11:00～16:00。人気のフレーバーは公式Instagram（@comecome_chiffon_）で告知。店独自の小麦不使用／グルテンフリー表記は確認できず。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Rice-flour chiffon specialty shop; no wheat products found, but no published cross-contact protocol — celiac should confirm no wheat is handled and ask about baking powder / shared equipment.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Chiffon cakes are egg-based; dairy-free (organic soy milk, rice oil) but not vegan.

positives.text: Made with rice flour (米粉), organic soy milk (有機豆乳), rice oil (米油) and unrefined cane sugar (きび砂糖) — no wheat flour named in the shop's description.

places.name: Mai+Chie Kitchen (まいちえキッチン)
places.gf_detail: This is an all-vegan organic takeout café, and Vegewel tags it both グルテンフリー and ヴィーガン, but the venue makes no own gluten-free or wheat-free (小麦不使用) claim in any Japanese source — its own description is simply "全てヴィーガンのオーガニックテイクアウトカフェです" (an entirely vegan organic takeout café). Some rice-flour (米粉) baked goods and rice-based bowls may be naturally gluten-free, but the menu prominently features wheat dishes — ヴィーガン唐揚げ (vegan karaage, wheat coating) and 汁なし坦々麺 (brothless tantan noodles, wheat noodles) — and the shop operates inside a shared kitchen (ミタキシェアキッチン), so cross-contact is a real concern. Because there is only a third-party Vegewel GF tag and no dedicated wheat-free preparation, a celiac diner must ask item-by-item and should not assume any dish is safe.
places.vegan_detail: The venue self-describes as "全てヴィーガンのオーガニックテイクアウトカフェ" — an entirely vegan, all-plant-based organic takeout café. Everything on the menu (breads, baked goods, karaage, tantan noodles, Buddha bowls, drinks) is vegan, so plant-based diners have no animal-product concerns here.
places.hours_raw: Fri–Sun 11:00–16:00 (closed Mon–Thu; also closed on days the owner is out at markets/events)
places.neighborhood: 広島市西区三滝本町 (Mitaki-honmachi, Nishi-ku), near Mitaki Station
places.cuisine: Vegan organic takeout café — plant-based bread, baked goods, bowls & drinks
places.notes: [GF] Vegewel lists this all-vegan takeout café under both gluten-free and vegan, but the venue itself makes no wheat-free claim, so treat the GF tag as unverified. It shares a communal kitchen and openly sells wheat items (vegan karaage, tantan wheat noodles), so cross-contact flour is present. A celiac should ask which items are 米粉 (rice-flour) based and accept that no dish is prepared in a certified gluten-free environment; naturally rice-based bowls are the safest bet but confirm each time.

chef_bio.background: Mai+Chie Kitchen (まいちえキッチン) is a small owner-run vegan takeout café that opened on 14 June 2025 inside the ミタキシェアキッチン (Mitaki Shared Kitchen) at 三滝本町2丁目1-8 in Nishi-ku, Hiroshima, about a five-minute walk from Mitaki Station on the JR Kabe Line. The owner operates it as an all-vegan, organic concept, describing the shop as "全てヴィーガンのオーガニックテイクアウトカフェ" and selling plant-based breads, baked goods, bowls and drinks. Because it works out of a shared kitchen, it is open only Friday through Sunday (and closes on days the owner is out selling at markets and events). The café also appears on delivery platforms such as Wolt and Uber Eats and posts through the Instagram handle @maichie.vegan ("Hiroshima Vegan Cafe").
chef_bio.philosophy: Fully plant-based, organic, health-oriented home cooking offered as accessible takeout.
chef_bio.specialty: Vegan organic bread and baked goods, plus plant-based comfort dishes like vegan karaage, brothless tantan noodles and organic Buddha bowls.

anecdotes.text: The shop opened on 14 June 2025 and runs out of a communal shared kitchen, which is why it trades only on weekends and around the owner's market appearances.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Vegewel と食べログの日本語情報より：本人の説明は「全てヴィーガンのオーガニックテイクアウトカフェです」。三滝シェアキッチン内で2025年6月14日開店、金〜日のみ営業、現金のみ、テイクアウト専門。グルテンフリーの独自表記はなく、Vegewelのタグのみ。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Operates inside a shared communal kitchen (ミタキシェアキッチン) and bakes/sells wheat products, so shared surfaces and equipment mean flour cross-contact is likely; no dedicated gluten-free prep is claimed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No tamari/wheat-free soy sauce is specified; standard Japanese shoyu (which contains wheat) should be assumed in seasoned dishes like the tantan noodles and karaage unless the owner confirms otherwise.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Entirely vegan kitchen — no animal products used — so there is no meat/dairy cross-contact risk for plant-based diners.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Vegewel notes English-speaking staff and an English menu are available, which may help a celiac diner ask about ingredients, but no formal allergy protocol is documented.

positives.text: All-vegan organic concept with some rice-flour (米粉) style baked goods and rice-based Buddha bowls that can be naturally gluten-free if confirmed.

places.name: ONE EARTH & lovefamcook (ワンアース&ラブファムクック)
places.gf_detail: This organic cafe is tagged グルテンフリー on Vegewel ("グルテンフリーメニューあり"), but no gluten-free or wheat-free (小麦不使用) claim is made by the venue itself on its own channels, so the rating is capped at options. Naturally gluten-free items include the organic smoothies, organic coffee and 有機よもぎ茶; however the signature dishes lean wheat — the ヴィーガンブリトー uses a wheat tortilla, the ベジサンド uses 21-grain bread, and the vegan/moringa curry roux is unverified for wheat. Wheat bread and burritos are handled in the same small kitchen, so cross-contact is a real risk for celiacs and any GF meal should be confirmed with staff in person.
places.vegan_detail: Concept is 口から食べるもので未来を考える (organic, additive-free, plant-forward). There are clearly vegan dishes — ヴィーガンカレーライス, ヴィーガンブリトー, アサイーボウル (made with agave, no honey/yogurt) and all organic smoothies are vegan. However the venue is not fully vegan: the ハイパワープレート includes boiled egg and house chicken ham, and the ライトセット salad uses a yogurt-based dressing, so vegans should specify their order.
places.hours_raw: Wed 9:00–17:00 / Thu–Sun 11:30–17:00 / closed Mon & Tue. Listings vary: Vegewel shows 11:30–19:00 (L.O. 18:30), a note article shows 11:30–17:30 (L.O. 17:00).
places.neighborhood: 三川町・八丁堀 (Hatchobori / Hondori downtown, Naka-ku, Hiroshima; ~23 min walk from Hiroshima Station)
places.cuisine: Organic cafe — vegan/vegetarian curry, burritos, sandwiches, acai bowls, smoothies
places.notes: [GF] — Vegewel lists a gluten-free menu, but ONE EARTH makes no wheat-free claim of its own, so treat as options only. Confidently GF choices are the organic smoothies, organic coffee and mugwort tea; the curry, burrito (wheat tortilla) and 21-grain sandwich are wheat or unverified. Wheat bread/burritos share the kitchen, and seasoned side dishes likely use ordinary (wheat) soy sauce — no tamari mentioned. A celiac should confirm each dish with staff and not assume the curry roux is safe.

chef_bio.background: Owner Masayuki Kamimae studied dance in California, where organic food was part of his everyday life. Returning to Japan he found organic products hard to access and felt his health suffer from increased food additives, which motivated him to open ONE EARTH so future generations could eat well. He designed the basement space himself, marked by a green sign, in the Mikawa-cho / Namiki-dori area of central Hiroshima. The shop pairs the ONE EARTH organic cafe with the lovefamcook plant-based kitchen.
chef_bio.philosophy: 口から食べるもので未来を考える — thinking about Japan's future through what we put in our mouths, extending the healthy lifespan of children and adults alike through organic, additive-free food.
chef_bio.specialty: Organic additive-free plant-forward cafe fare — moringa/vegan curry, acai bowls, organic smoothies

anecdotes.text: Kamimae says he does not want organic treated as something special, but wants customers to embrace it with familiarity in daily life.
His time studying dance in California, where he ate organic routinely, is the origin story for founding the cafe after returning to an additive-heavy diet in Japan.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Vegewel掲載ページ、japanese-heart.com紹介記事、note.com旅ライター記事より。オーナーは上前昌之氏。カリフォルニアでダンスを学びオーガニック生活を経験、帰国後に添加物の多さと健康不安からONE EARTHを開業。地下の店舗を自ら手掛け、並木通りエリアに立地。コンセプトは『口から食べるもので未来を考える』。ヴィーガン・オーガニックが中心で、グルテンフリーはVegewelのタグのみで店独自の小麦不使用表記は確認できず。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat burritos (wheat tortilla) and 21-grain sandwich bread are prepared in the same small basement kitchen as any GF-eligible items, creating cross-contact risk; no dedicated GF prep is claimed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Seasoned Japanese side dishes (e.g. simmered eggplant/potato in the plate sets) are described but no tamari/wheat-free soy sauce is mentioned, so standard wheat-containing soy sauce should be assumed unless confirmed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Egg and house chicken ham (ハイパワープレート) and yogurt dressing (ライトセット) are used in the same kitchen; vegan dishes exist but are not prepared in a segregated area.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published statement on celiac/allergy handling; the gluten-free designation comes only from the Vegewel directory tag, not the venue.

positives.text: All-organic, additive-free, pesticide-free ingredient sourcing with genuine vegan entrees and smoothies, and a Vegewel gluten-free menu tag.

places.name: Komeko Udon Undo (米粉うどん製麺所 undo／あんど)
places.gf_detail: A rice-flour udon noodle shop and micro-factory where the noodles are 100% domestic rice flour and the whole menu is built to be wheat-free. The shop states 「小麦粉不使用」 (no wheat flour used) and 「出汁やタレなどもすべてグルテンフリー」 (the dashi, sauces and everything are all gluten-free), which makes it one of the rare celiac-safe SAVORY sit-down meals in central Hiroshima. Because the venue is a dedicated 米粉 (rice-flour) noodle operation rather than a mixed wheat kitchen, cross-contact risk is low, but the shop has not published a formal dedicated-fryer / allergen-protocol statement, so a celiac diner should still confirm at the counter that their bowl shares no equipment with any wheat item and that soy-based tare is the GF one.
places.vegan_detail: The parent okonomiyaki brand undo opened this factory partly in response to repeated vegan and gluten-free requests from foreign tourists, and offers vegan options (e.g. a vegetable okonomiyaki using rice-flour noodles/rice paper, ~¥1,200). Not every udon is vegan (mentaiko-mayo and some dashi contain fish/animal products), so vegans should confirm which broth and toppings are plant-based.
places.hours_raw: 11:30–17:00 (Thursdays until 15:00); closed every Tuesday. New menu items released on Fridays.
places.neighborhood: 中区大手町 (Otemachi, Naka-ku, Hiroshima)
places.cuisine: Rice-flour udon noodle shop / gluten-free noodle factory
places.notes: [GF] A dedicated rice-flour (米粉) udon shop 2 min from Hondori station and near the Atomic Bomb Dome — one of the very few places in Hiroshima where a celiac can eat a hot savory noodle bowl. Noodles are 100% rice flour and the shop advertises all dashi/tare as gluten-free. No published dedicated-fryer statement, so confirm no shared wheat equipment and choose the GF tare when ordering. Opened March 2025 (in-store dining from late May 2025) — verify current hours via the @undo_factory Instagram before visiting.

roles: owner-operator (株式会社ユーカラー / Yo Karaaa Inc.)
chef_bio.background: The shop is run by 株式会社ユーカラー (Yo Karaaa Inc.), which operates the okonomiyaki restaurant undo inside the 'Hiroshima Okonomiyaki Yokocho /5' complex. Since undo opened in August 2023, many foreign tourists asked for vegan and gluten-free options, which led the company to develop 100% rice-flour udon and open this dedicated 米粉うどん製麺所 (rice-flour udon factory) in Otemachi in March 2025. The factory both supplies gluten-free noodles for the okonomiyaki shop and serves udon dishes on-site.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make Hiroshima's noodle and okonomiyaki culture accessible to gluten-free and vegan visitors by replacing wheat with domestic rice flour.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% domestic rice-flour udon with a chewy, rice-fragrant texture; gluten-free okonomiyaki.

anecdotes.text: The factory floor is visible through glass, and a reservation-based space lets visitors try hand-pulling rice-flour udon.
The venture grew directly out of vegan/gluten-free demand from inbound tourists at the sister okonomiyaki shop undo.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese guide (bm-peekaboo.com) and the company press release (PR Times) confirm the shop uses 100% domestic rice flour, states 小麦粉不使用, and describes all dashi/tare as gluten-free; the shop opened in Otemachi in 2025 and is operated by Yo Karaaa Inc. 姉妹店の広島お好み焼きundoは2023年8月開店。ガラス越しに製麺工場が見え、予約制の手打ち体験がある。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Dedicated rice-flour udon operation using 100% domestic rice flour; the shop states 「小麦粉不使用」 (no wheat flour used). No formal cross-contact/allergen protocol has been published, so confirm equipment is not shared with any wheat item.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The shop states 「出汁やタレなどもすべてグルテンフリー」 (the dashi, sauces and tare are all gluten-free), implying wheat-free soy/tare; confirm the specific bowl's tare is the gluten-free one.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Vegan options exist (vegetable okonomiyaki ~¥1,200), but shared broths may contain fish dashi; vegans should confirm the broth.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The business was created specifically to serve gluten-free and vegan tourist demand, so staff are accustomed to allergen questions; no written allergen policy published.

positives.text: 100% domestic rice-flour noodles; all dashi and sauces advertised as gluten-free — a genuine celiac-safe savory meal.

places.name: Yamaichi Bekkan (割烹旅館 山一別館)
places.gf_detail: A kappo-style kaiseki ryokan right by the Miyajima ferry terminal that will convert its multi-course meals to gluten-free ON REQUEST. Its own site states 「可能な限り、全てのお料理をグルテンフリー仕様に変更できます。また、各種アレルギー対応のお食事も可能ですので、事前にお電話にて詳細をお知らせくださいませ」— i.e. as far as possible all dishes can be remade gluten-free, and various allergies accommodated, but you MUST phone in advance. Because this is a shared ryokan kitchen (wheat soy sauce, tempura, wheat-thickened sauces are all normally present) rather than a dedicated GF facility, this caps at 'options': the conversion depends on advance notice and careful kitchen handling, so a celiac should call, explain celiac disease (セリアック病／小麦アレルギー), and confirm cross-contact precautions.
places.vegan_detail: Offers a fully plant-based vegetarian/vegan kaiseki course in which vegetables are styled to resemble meat and fish, with even the seasonings kept animal-free; the venue explicitly markets to vegetarian, vegan and halal-conscious guests. Groups can mix — some diners take the vegan course while others eat the standard meal. Arrange in advance so the plant-based course can be prepared.
places.hours_raw: Kappo ryokan and restaurant; dining by reservation. Check-in 15:00–17:00, check-out by 10:00. GF/allergy and vegan meals require an advance phone call (TEL 0829-44-0700).
places.neighborhood: 廿日市市宮島町 (Miyajima Island, Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima)
places.notes: [GF] One of the very few places ON Miyajima island that will accommodate a celiac diner — a kaiseki ryokan near the ferry pier that remakes courses gluten-free with advance notice. It is NOT dedicated GF (shared kitchen with wheat soy sauce, tempura, etc.), so this is an advance-notice 'options' spot: phone ahead (0829-44-0700), state celiac disease, and confirm tamari substitution and cross-contact care. High value because GF-friendly options on the island itself are scarce — most day-trippers have to eat back in Miyajimaguchi or Hiroshima city.

chef_bio.background: 山一別館 (Yamaichi Bekkan) is a traditional kappo (割烹) ryokan and dining house located at the entrance to Miyajima, directly in front of the Itsukushima ferry terminal. It presents itself as a place to share Japan's culinary excellence with international guests, actively catering to vegetarian, vegan, halal-conscious and gluten-free diners. The kitchen develops plant-based kaiseki in which vegetables are shaped to look like meat and fish, and offers to convert standard courses to gluten-free as much as possible on advance request.
chef_bio.philosophy: Share the best of Japanese cuisine with every guest by accommodating diverse dietary needs (vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free) through advance consultation.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal kaiseki; a vegetable-based course styled to imitate meat and fish; allergy-adapted meals by prior arrangement.

anecdotes.text: Vegetables are arranged and cooked to resemble meat and fish so plant-based guests still get a full kaiseki experience.
Mixed groups can order — some the vegetarian course, others the standard meal — at the same table.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The venue's own vegetarian.html page states it can remake dishes gluten-free as much as possible and accommodate various allergies with advance phone notice, offers a full plant-based (vegan) kaiseki course styled to look like meat and fish, lets mixed groups order different courses at one table, and markets to vegetarian, vegan and halal guests (halal-certified meats). Located at 宮島町1162-4 by the ferry terminal, TEL 0829-44-0700.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared ryokan kitchen, not a dedicated GF facility; GF is achieved by remaking dishes on request. Confirm cross-contact precautions when phoning ahead.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard kaiseki uses wheat-based soy sauce; the venue says dishes can be changed to gluten-free 「可能な限り、全てのお料理をグルテンフリー仕様に変更できます」 — confirm tamari/wheat-free seasoning is used for your meal.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dedicated plant-based kaiseki course prepared to be animal-free including seasonings; still a shared kitchen, so arrange in advance.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The venue explicitly requests advance phone contact to arrange allergy/GF meals: 「事前にお電話にて詳細をお知らせくださいませ」 (TEL 0829-44-0700).

positives.text: One of the only GF-accommodating dining spots on Miyajima island itself; willing to convert full courses to gluten-free with notice.
